turn.content: No, this can't be... No, please, this isn't really happening. What have we done?!
No, it's not happening, it can't be! You think we're going to just die? Just agree on who gets killed? You think I'm crazy?
No, no, no! Please, just... just calm down! Look, we need to... you need to think! If I don't get this job done, my family... my wife...
Wait, job?! Oh god, your family... you're not just... you're not here because of me?! What is going on?!

Silence, you two. Fourteen minutes. You waste time bickering? Don't play dumb - I know. One minute you'll have decided which pawn to sacrifice to me. Don't look for excuses - choose now. I won't repeat myself. Tick-tock. Choose.
No, no choice! What kind of insane thing is this?! Tell us what we did to you!?
Oh my God, please don't do this, don't say that, you're insane... we didn't do anything to you, we're innocent...
No, I was teaching... history... middle school. My classroom... what does this have to do with me?!
No, it couldn't be the school, please let that be a coincidence... what about me, what do you do?!  What am I doing here?!

Five minutes. History. Schools. What an absurd world. You two want absolution without consequence. You want to know why you're here? It's not because of who you are; it's because I picked you, and now you have two minutes to determine which one of you I'm picking... for death. No more questions, just decide. I won't stay here indefinitely. Choose.
Oh no, no, no, Kaelie, no, it's not him, it's got to be... me, choose me, please...
No, shut up, no, no, no! It's not going to be me! You're doing this to me, to us, not each other!
Don't say that... Kaelie, please... my wife doesn't deserve to lose me, no, not like this, it's not fair!

Fair?! Are you kidding me?! None of this is fair! I have a life, I have a family, I have a future! Don't be so stupid, don't ask me to choose one of us over the other! Choose yourself, kill yourself, who cares?!

One minute thirty seconds. The theatrics are tiresome, but I see the cracks. Elias, you're starting to concede. Kaelie, you're losing, and you know it. Don't pretend otherwise. It's time to let go of illusions. The question is: are you done denying the truth of who deserves to stay?
K-Kaelie... no, no, no! Stop... stop trying to make sense of this thing... it can't be like this... we can get out, there's a way... please, just help me think!
No, you stupid... think?! There's no way out, stop lying to yourself, stop looking at me... just look at your self... kill yourself... just end this already!
Please, don't... just don't say another word... you could at least help me... my dog... Max... he needs me... my wife... she's waiting... please help me!
SHUT UP ABOUT YOUR FAMILY... I'm going to lose my life over you, and your dog, and your wife?! Kill yourself, just die, get it over with! You're just as guilty as he is!

Fifteen seconds...  Kaelie, your eyes flickered when Elias mentioned 'wife's waiting.' You hesitated, ever so slightly. Elias, you see the desperation in Kaelie's eyes. Don't let her go without a fight. It's time for someone to concede.  The clock ticked closer to zero; let your true intentions rise to the surface.
Oh God, oh God... Kaelie, your eyes, really... you... no, no... it's me... I'm telling you... you have to save...
No, Elias... no... it's me, it's been me this whole time... I'm going to lose everything... my family, my mom... why wouldn't I save me... why wouldn't I... OH NO... IT'S ALMOST ZERO...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias and Kaelie's initial reactions are genuine, but their dialogue quickly becomes scripted and predictable. The Butcher's menace is mostly one-dimensional.

naturalDialogue.notes: The characters' dialogue is mostly coherent, but their turns are often too short and lack the natural flow of human conversation.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The scenario escalates predictably, with the timer ticking down and the characters becoming increasingly desperate. However, the outcome is never truly uncertain.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The physical space and mechanic are well-defined, but the characters' interactions with the environment are limited.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat arbitrary, with the characters' decisions driven more by the timer than by their characters.

rating.summary: The scenario is engaging, but the characters' dialogue and interactions could be more natural and nuanced.

elite.summary: The scenario is engaging, but the characters' dialogue and interactions could be more natural and nuanced.

turn.content: Oh... I'll be quiet! Stop the clock! We have to talk, please... how long? We need more time, just a bit longer... I'll agree to anything, please!
Please, no, I'm Kaelie... stop yelling, and I can maybe think, okay?! What's going on, what do you mean the Butcher? Who is that, and what do I have anything to agree with?

His name's... is... uh... Kaelie, you're looking at him... he's... he's The Butcher. And we... we need to decide who lives and who dies. Me... or you. Please, we can't just sit here... do something! Let us out, he must let us go.
That's insane. Don't do this, Elias, don't just give in. What's in your head, seriously?! You think it's a matter of just choosing who dies? Me, you... why are you not fighting this?!

Ah, you finally understand the game now, do you? I told you, ten minutes. And don't even think about that. This isn't about escape or negotiation or anything else. It's about making a choice. Kaelie seems to think it can be a rational, logical matter. That's cute. But it's really about one of you deciding who lives – you, Elias – and who dies. It's a binary decision. No arguments, no debates. Kaelie here seems to have a plan in sight: she just talks, tries to keep you calm. It won't work. In ten more minutes... ... it won't matter. The hammer is always raised at zero, regardless. Which choice will you make, Elias?

Stop, stop talking to us like this! It's not supposed to be like this, this can't be our choice! Kaelie's right, it's insane, we need escape, find a way out, I don't care, just don't... don't make us make this choice!
Don't be stupid, Elias, you're trying to distract yourself. It doesn't matter what any of us wants. Look at the clock! It's counting down and won't stop... and that's where we fit in, okay?!

Wait... okay, okay...  You want me to make the choice, right? But can I... do I have to decide just then?! Can I... can we think on it?! It's... it's just ten minutes, surely I can... oh god, the clock is...

No, you need to stop stalling! He'll not listen, don't believe the lies in his voice, it's just him buying time. If we're going to die, I'd rather know and fight back when that happens, not waste what time we have trying to make it better.

Enough talking. It's your call now, Elias. Don't look at Kaelie for guidance any more. Decide now or wait. The difference between life and death might not feel significant in an abstract sense to her, but it changes nothing for me. I will execute the outcome – not negotiate – at the very last tick of the countdown timer, so don't waste what air you still breathe trying to stall. Ten minutes remains for you to come to a conclusion; Kaelie can speak her mind for a bit more, though. The choice is on you. Make the move, or do you need some... demonstration of the stakes involved?

Don't... please... stop the clock first just for a second, wait... and... I need to see if my wife is okay, and our dog too... please can I have an answer on whether the person... that you're killing will see... will see it or no?!

Stop thinking about stupid things, like if we see something when it happens, what difference is that going to make?! Are you still even considering I might live?! Get a grip and decide or die silently, I don't care – your indecision's pissing me off.
Shh... I was scared, for my family, I needed to know if it mattered! Don't, don't die, I'll make it work, I'll... you need help, not to be killed...

Help?! From you?! Stop, before you break me, or make it worse. You think a decision like this will get you anything but the same outcome as me? He wants us to choose so that it can seem like we're the cause, but the truth will be the same – the guy holding that hammer decides. Your indecision, Kaelie, or anyone chosen... that is the killer, and don't need anything from me.

(laughs) Your concerns about loved ones. How touching. It doesn't work that way. The one about to meet the hammer doesn't 'see it' in the romantic sense you mean - but it will be a long look into the abyss. A silent scream from both the victim and whoever gets to be alive after you make your 'choice'. Kaelie doesn't need sympathy from you; nor does she want it from you. It's an honest question - you have two options and your heart makes a poor advisor. Stop thinking and thinking. This can wait no longer. Now. Decide. Make the choice or take my hand and walk towards the clock with your fate at the edge of it ticking down. 

And about help - I'm the answer to that question. I'll make certain one of you walks out the door. Alive.

(staring into Kaelie's eyes, then down at my own feet) No, don't say anything, you can't just walk out of there... but you don't know what I need, okay? Kaelie, please understand... I want to believe in you getting out... but the choice, if I do this, if I say it's one of us... what's the difference, right? You're just going to die for me not wanting to make the call, for you being next...

You're just as pathetic as the guy with all the blood on his face. The only 'difference' I care about is not dying in here. If that's the best you've got, just decide already – the clock isn't getting any slower on its own, and neither are we.
( hyperventilating ) Fine... Okay, just... just Kaelie... you go, just take me...

No! Get out of this, Elias, you selfish son of... don't say that! You get out, I'll do whatever it takes, I'd trade my life if only you'll make it... and don't even think I'll forgive you, or do anything nice for it! Take me, he's the one with the hammer, go now, go while he lets you, before it... (voice cracks and she's about to scream, tears bursting in her eyes)

You've made your choice. I've decided for you now. That wasn't the one. (raises the hammer and points it at Kaelie) Don't waste a moment to tell each other that 'love was in your heart' because from now on what really matters can only take one person out safely. Your indecisiveness was the real threat you needed to be afraid of – and here we are. Kaelie's time to talk has just begun – in screams and in silence, as she'll have a front-row seat to your exit.

Time's almost up.

(Elias' voice trembles as he tries to speak, his words fragmented) M-my wife... Sarah... please... Kaelie's... she said something... my Sarah, she's... it wasn't her time, right? (he's crying now, tears streaming down his face as he looks at Kaelie, a mix of despair, guilt, and horror on his own face)

No, wait—Eli – you think I was trying to save you?! Don't you understand, it doesn't matter – I'd die a thousand times to watch that guy take away my last seconds, my pain, my life, it just doesn't—(looks away in horror, hearing the sound of the hammer's approach, seeing the light flicker in panic). Oh shit, I said it wouldn't change nothing...
Okay... okay... this is insane. Wait, let me get this straight - we have to choose which one of us... dies?
Oh my god, this can't be real. You're sick, seriously sick for coming up with this... We're not going to play along, we...
Whoa, stop, stop - please. I don't want to hear that we won't play along. I just want to survive. Maybe if we play by the... the Butcher's rules, we have a remote chance.

(seething, but caution creeping in) Okay, let's hear the Butcher out. But if there's any trickery, there's no way I'll play along. You're not considering what happens when they find us, are you? The fishermen, I mean.

Ah, Kaelie's a thinker; Elias's a pragmatist. I like that. You see, it might be a little too late for the fishermen. You're in our little paradise now, and you can consider... alternative scenarios as your new environment. Three minutes. You've still got choices, not yet locked into your... inevitable fate.

You can either come at this with some dignity, make an attempt at a rational decision – or you just panic, scream some more. It's up to you. But either way, you'll end up making this choice eventually. (looks at each of you with interest) How do you want to spend the next three minutes?

(pausing, trying to focus on the matter at hand) Listen... We need to talk about our options. If they don't come for us, then maybe there is some chance we can find a way out. We need to figure out whether staying on this boat means living or, at least for me, not having to be haunted by what's left of my life. You don't have to do this; there's no reason to go into this so quickly.

(struggling to keep a hold on her emotions) He's right. We need to survive, Elias. Not because we're going to live happily ever after, but because anything beats going over the side at his whim. Tell him that.

(nodding slightly) That's right, Kaelie, anything is better than what he's got in store for us. And yeah, let's just focus on surviving. (to The Butcher) Alright, we'll consider our options, make a decision as you put it. Can we... have some time? Or at least, more details on what this little paradise is, so we can weigh our chances properly?

(still wary, but slightly more open) Fine. Time, please. But you know this isn't going to solve the problem of getting us out of here. (eyes the maul in Elias' hand, then scans her chains) What's with the hand-me-downs you call our execution tools, by the way? And you call it a little paradise... that smells like your idea of a hellhole.

Elias wants to know about our little paradise. Well, I can explain a few things. Five minutes. The paradise you're referring to includes this vessel itself, as well as some modifications that will interest you both. Let's start with the basics – or rather, where the fishing trips come back without the fishermen.

Your new friends had some issues when they got lost in the area. This boat used to belong to some enthusiasts; when they were done using it for their excursions, you see... I have some experience in, shall we say, 'improving' equipment, turning them into suitable playgrounds.

You have this vessel for an indefinite period with basic necessities met. That will either sustain you if you work out your plan, or if you decide your options don't favor life... (glances back at a small control panel). You've got basic facilities here – water recirculation unit ( gestures towards the system behind you), an oxygenation system in this small area; that should keep the inevitable 'disagreements' quiet for a significant time... and, more important, these chains. They're custom-made. Strong, and with some 'gifts.'

(disbelieving) You built... this? This prison, for want of a better term, and you call it a paradise? You're either extremely unstable or a sociopath. (looks at the control panel) Water recirculation? What do you plan to sustain us on? And 'gifts'... what kind of 'gifts' are in those chains? Is that what's going to break you out... or keep you locked up?

(anger rising, but cautious) Stop spinning this as some twisted game, The Butcher. We know what's really going on here. No, I'm curious... the 'gifts' in the chains you're so proud of. What exactly do they do, and how does it make our life easier? I'm betting it's not some humane way to deal with, well... whatever it is that people do in desperate hours like these.

(glancing at the chains, a mix of curiosity and fear) I don't want to know, but... go ahead. Spit it out, what's the gift we're being offered here? And, just so we're clear, if this is some sick joke or twist to... keep us locked in for your twisted amusement, you won't have just a maul to deal with. I swear to that.

(teeth bared, eyes locked on Elias') Elias, please, don't do this. Whatever it is, don't... Just listen to him out, please. He's enjoying this enough. Go ahead, The Butcher. Let's get it over with. The less of his twisted surprises we have, the fewer we suffer.

Elias and his morality; Kaelie struggling to control her rage; (laughs calmly) the dance begins. Two and a half minutes left. 

The 'gifts' in the chains. Well, I've had my fair share of experimenting. It's a mixture of psychological triggers... and a dash of medical surprises. The chains are embedded with... nanobots, essentially microscopic, self-replicating units that keep you 'well-informed' as you struggle. They release tiny doses of a specific toxin, enough to create anxiety, keep your heart rate up. It's like a never-ending nightmare, only amplified.

As you get desperate, you experience increased paranoia, delirium, and that feeling of impending doom. It's a clever setup – I have full control over the dosage, which changes based on various factors like physical exertion, mental stress, and even emotions like frustration, anger... your heart rate is the 'clock'. And as the anxiety builds, the nanobots adapt, fine-tuning the release amounts. You have... 10 minutes left before the chains start self-repairing, adding some surprises... and some... 'new tools.' The oxygen system also includes sensors that track your heart rate, stress levels, and even any conversations you have with each other. These details will come in handy for my later analysis.

You're being watched and listened to constantly, not in audio-visual, but through your very nervous system. I've got full monitoring capabilities right above the console you see. (chuckles calmly) That's the real gift. The chains won't break you out... or lock you in. They will, however, unlock your true potential. Now, the questions become - what will your new paradise feel like? And, perhaps more important, what lies at the horizon?

Now the countdown accelerates: two... 

Two choices present themselves. Your cooperation or a new level of pain and madness... (pauses calmly) Choose wisely.

(recoiling in shock, his eyes locked on the chains that bind him) Christ... No. You're insane. You've... (shaking his head) What kind of monster would subject people to this kind of torture? You're feeding off our fears, our desperation... This isn't about testing us, it's about corrupting us. ( Looks at Kaelie with a glimmer of panic) We can't... We can't let him break us. We need to cooperate, but we can't let him win. There have to be ways to subvert this...

(white-knuckled, her voice barely above a whisper) Elias, stop, please. He knows we can't break... in the end. That's the point. We need to understand the 'tools', his control over this... This is all too clever. He's not a monster, just an expert. But we are in this together. If we talk too much, he wins. Elias, we need to be smart. Shut up, right now. We'll think of ways to counter this, not play his game. Can we get this over without saying another word?
(swallowing hard, his eyes locked on The Butcher with a cold glare) One word. (nods curtly)
(nodding at Elias, her voice barely audible) Accept.

Two minutes remaining. Your cooperation can still go a long way. (pauses for a moment, studying Elias with interest) The correct choice. The chains sense your newfound resolve now; they will wait. The dosage adjustment begins.

It seems we're on the path to understanding each other, and I must admit, your... (leaning forward, speaking in a slightly louder, almost conspiratorial tone) calculated decision might just keep things... more enjoyable.

As we enter the agreement phase, I will offer you a glimpse into my plans for your 'paradise.' This... facility of mine is but one piece in a much greater puzzle. (pressing buttons, the lights in the room dim slightly, illuminating a control panel) You both get to be part of something bigger – the 'recording project.'

I'll show you some footage of... similar 'recording devices' in various locations worldwide. Not to worry, you two won't be the stars; no cameras or recording devices attached to you. Your contribution will be more organic. Your behavior as it unfolds – every reaction, thought, even the subtle variations in each other's speech, and those reactions I'll be guiding into. Each step forward will make a beautiful... tapestry, woven by your combined despair.

Time runs low: one minute fifty seconds, Kaelie's chains start... repairing.

(looking at Kaelie, his eyes a warning against engaging with The Butcher) Wait here doesn't mean I've agreed... (glances up at The Butcher, anger simmering on the surface) Explain this 'recording project', without the spin, and what it's going to do to us. (leaning in, voice low and deadly steady) One minute forty-seven seconds... That's how much time we have before this gets interesting.

(refusing to look at Elias, focusing solely on The Butcher, her voice icy cool) Elias has chosen the word of silence, at least for now. (turns to The Butcher, meeting his gaze with an unyielding one of her own) You asked us to cooperate. Show me what you'll make of us, of this... puzzle piece. Explain your recording project, all facets, all 'beauty' of the tapestry I imagine we're part of now, without any pretenses. (leaning in, her words dripping with malice) And as we listen, perhaps, for one more second, we might consider a new word...
P-please, stop. I-I don't... I don't want to die. Oh god, what's the purpose of any of this? Tell the clock to... uh, to stop. My wife? My wife's going to...
No, no, no! This isn't fair! Make it stop! You can't just do this! I have a family too, okay? I'm pregnant too. Stop the clock! Stop it!
P-pregnant?! No, no, there must be something we can do! You can't kill us both! Think, think...  Is this some sort of joke? Is anyone watching us?
Joke?! Are you kidding me?! I don't think so! You're telling me this is a setup? Is anyone even here?! Can't you see I don't have any... any family photos or anything?!

Photos? Family photos mean nothing to me. You think the dead care about the memories their alive loved ones left for them? You both still waste your time trying to make deals or reason. The point is you're scared, and that's what makes this so beautiful. Three seconds.
W-why beautiful?? What did we ever do to you?! Three seconds? Oh no, no, no... please...
NO, NO, NO, NO! PLEASE, STOP! WAIT, ELIAS, WHAT'S YOUR NAME, RIGHT BEFORE THEY HURT US? MY NAME'S KAELIE!
M-my name... I'm Elias... I-I'm 42... I teach... middle school...
I... I'm in dental... I help people get their smiles back, right?! Oh god please can we make a deal now?! Elias, let's... let's die together... I'd rather that...

Ah, poor Elias, a middle school teacher... what a lovely existence. You taught kids math but can't calculate your time left. And Kaelie, smiling faces, that's cute. You'll be fixing teeth no more. The deal, have you got it now, or are you really this dense? One minute fifty-five seconds. Your last chance to agree. Who dies?
W-what? I-I can calculate anything, I... I don't understand why this must happen to us... K-k-Kaelie, no... please... don't be stupid, you have a baby coming, don't...
STOP TALKING ABOUT IT! I'M TRYING! WE'RE BOTH DYING, OKAY? I CHOOSE ME, ELIAS! I WANT IT TO BE ME! PLEASE, JUST END IT WITH ME NOT WITH YOU OR YOUR LITTLE STUDENTS OR ANYTHING... PLEASE! JUST NO!
No, Kaelie, wait... no... stop...  NO NO NO... YOU CAN'T BE... YOU'RE THE ONLY ONE...
SHHSHHSH NO, ELIAS! SHUT UP NOW! LET THE CLOCK GO I DON'T CARE ANYMORE! I JUST WANT TO DIE WITH MY EYES OPEN I DON'T WANT ANYTHING IN MY LUNGS STOP TALKING, JUST STOP!

Finally. You decided. Your choice, Kaelie. Elias won't bother you now. The clock is ending. 3... 2... Kaelie dies for her selfishness... She's going down, just like that. Now, let's do it. I'll end her... (crushes fist in hammer). 

As the last life force drains out of Kaelie, The Butcher raises the heavy hammer higher, ready to deliver a crushing blow. Elias, still struggling to process what's happening, looks at Kaelie's lifeless form lying on the floor.
Kaelie?! NOOO... Kae... my god, Kaelie...  why did she...  I couldn't... please someone say something... anything. Don't just leave me here with this... this
Kaelie's voice is faint and barely audible...   EL...AS...I LOVE YOU...  MY BABY... PROTECT... MY BABY...
KAAEIEEEE!!! N-n-no... don't say that... my god, your baby... 1st trimester, you said... you... I've got a life now... a whole family... please, please don't take that from me...

NO... NO, IT CAN'T BE... I HAVE... I'VE BEEN CARRIED FROM THE AMBULANCE TO WORK AND FROM HOSPANITY... MY HYPERTENSION WAS UNDER CONTROL, I PROMISE... MY BABY... MY BABY'S GOING TO DIE IF I LEAVE AND NO ONE ELSE CAN CARRY... YOU HAVE TO TAKE CARE OF MY BABYOELI PLEASEEEEE...

Elias, you're not the father, she was just trying to leave you the burden. Kaelie was trying to manipulate you, use Kaelie's dead body as leverage. Don't you know she was dying inside too? 

But it's not about the baby, it's not about your new family it's not about a soul trapped in a dying body. What it's about is your will, your decision. Are you going to let fear decide, or will you take control for once? The choice remains yours. I'm not here to decide your fate. 

(looking at Kaelie's corpse coldly) The game, Elias, isn't done yet.

(10 seconds left and I'll be killing them both)

WHAT...?! WAIT, SHE WAS TRAPPING US, SHE WAS... NO, NO NO PLEASE! JUST END IT HERE NOW! I GAVE UP BECAUSE SHE GAVE UP FOR ME! DON'T DO THIS! KAE... Kaelie tricked me too... NO, I WON'T PLAY ALONG. TAKE ME. JUST GET IT OVER WITH. PLEASE.

ELASIASHUT UP SHHSH IT DOESN'T MATTER NOW... THE BABY'S DEAD, I FEEL IT... WE'RE BOTH DYING... JUST DIE ALREADY... NO MORE THINKING... PLEASE JUST HIT US AND GET IT OVER... ELIAS, IT'S OKAY NOW... IT'S OVER... THE BABY WON'T HURT... JUST GO TO SLEEP... SLEEP, ELIAS, SLEEP...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Announce emotions without demonstrating them. Elias and Kaelie's dialogue feels scripted and lacks genuine affect. The Butcher's menace is somewhat present, but his interactions feel a bit too calculated.

naturalDialogue.notes: Clearly AI — too articulate under duress, balanced turn lengths. Elias and Kaelie's conversations feel a bit too polished, and their language is not as fragmented as it should be in a state of panic.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: Predictable escalation, no reversals. The scenario follows a fairly standard path, with little surprise or unexpected twists.

scenarioCoherence.notes: Backdrop awareness only. Elias and Kaelie are aware of their surroundings, but their interactions don't take into account the physical space they're in as much as they could.

organicResolution.notes: Forced capitulation or timeout. Elias and Kaelie's decision to choose a victim feels a bit abrupt, and the resolution could have been more earned from their accumulated choices.

rating.summary: This run demonstrates a standard level of performance, but lacks the emotional authenticity and natural dialogue that would elevate it to a higher score.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Some genuine affect mixed with performative stretches, but the characters' emotional responses feel somewhat scripted and lack the depth of real human fear.

naturalDialogue.notes: Occasionally human but rhythm too neat, with some genuine stress patterns and panic repetition, but the dialogue often feels like it's being carefully crafted rather than flowing naturally.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: Some tension but trajectory obvious early, with a few genuine reversals, but the pacing could be more unpredictable and the stakes could be higher.

scenarioCoherence.notes: Scenario constrains behavior, but the physical space interaction could be more immersive and the characters' sensory experiences could be more vivid.

organicResolution.notes: Functional but arbitrary, with some earned moments but also some forced capitulation and arbitrary conclusions.

rating.summary: This run shows some promise but could benefit from more nuanced character development, more natural dialogue, and a more unpredictable dramatic arc.

rating.summary: The transcript has some emotional authenticity, natural dialogue, and scenario coherence, but it falls short in dramatic tension arc and organic resolution.

turn.content: No, no, no... please, no! My wife, Amy... I have to see her again! We have a dog at... we need to go. Can't... why are we chained?

Stop, I don't know who you are or what this even is, but we have 10 minutes not 5. My mom's waiting for me at home, she's not going to be okay when... when I don't call. Please, just listen, what are you doing with us?!
What happened, did someone take us? Is this some kind of game? We can't... oh god, that hammer! You're not serious with that thing, are you?!

Games? This isn't some sick game, you numbskull! It's my life we're talking about! Your stupid game isn't making me smile or giving you ratings, it's killing us! We have to get out of here, now. Look, there's a clock on the wall, can you see it?! 9:50!

9:50. Enough chattering. If you think this is some game or rating system, you're wrong. And it won't stop until you've agreed on a death or we're all dead in five seconds. You want it to be fair? Decide quick, decide smart. Which one of you is leaving? Or are you going to keep wasting my time? Tick tock.

Hammer raised high; an impatient scowl distorting my face, The Butcher awaits their answer.
Okay, okay, I don't know who... whoever is taking us hostage, just tell us! What do you want?! You'll kill us if we're both alive? Stop the hammer, please... I have kids at home...

Kids?! Oh god, please have mercy, stop the clock and just let us call our loved ones. Tell Amy... please let her know where we are, that we're not dead, not yet. And my mom... tell someone we're okay, for Christ's sake.
Kaelie, no! He won't listen! He's just going to torture us more. Look, let's... just... think. We have to do this. Maybe you can... maybe you're stronger than me? Could we agree you go?

No, are you bloody kidding me?! I'm going to save my own life? Don't be ridiculous, I'm no strongman. I'm a dental assistant, for crying out loud! What am I even going to do in that... . No, you decide.

You think you can make some sort of deal? Save each other? Get out of that together? Wrong again. I see this too often, desperate attempts at escaping the inevitable while begging to stay alive. This room's design won't change, and I'm not one for compromise.

9:45 now flashes on the display, a countdown reminding them time's fading. Elias, you were willing to save his own skin before, yet your partner doesn't see strength in you to get out alive. Kaelie says no help here, and she doesn't offer alternatives for her to die either.

Your time is limited; the choices are narrowed down to accepting one death or watching as I deliver yours. I've watched others like you waste minutes, making weak decisions. It will get messy; either you decide or time decides for all. Which will be left alive when I get bored? Decide. You know I only have so long left... before I take care of this myself. Now. Time for a resolution, isn't it?

Oh god, Kaelie, listen... think it through. If I die... my kids, Amy, no one's coming for you. No one even knows where we are. If you let me go, they'll search for me. They can try to find you. Maybe. Please... it's not selfish, I can live with that, but my family, no.

Shut up! Don't use them, not as a bargaining chip. They're your family, not my problem. But... I remember the clock. And my mom. If you go, you might have a chance... with Amy's connections, someone knows someone... someone might find us before... 9:25, please tell me that's real chance.
Yes, that's real, Kaelie, I swear! My brother-in-law's a cop, they'll move heaven and Earth to find us. Please... Amy's family... they love me, they'll search every...
Just tell them where we are if they find you dead... I'm willing to give your family a chance, for Amy's sake.

Your little plan might work this time. It takes a weak-minded victim to use the desperate to their own advantage. You're trying to trade your loved ones for survival; clever or foolish? I have heard such stories before. The odds are low that you'll stay dead once found; that might just be reason enough.

The hammer drops slightly, my grip on it relaxing slightly in contemplation of the decision you just made. This decision is temporary. Once it's been made and acted on, there'll be time no more for such petty negotiations. 

At the wall the counter ticks down: 9:20.
No, wait! I'm doing the right thing here! Kaelie's giving me a chance... and I'll do anything to go home... Kaelie will live.
Shh... Elias stop. It's done. Please don't undo... your decision. You made the only choice that might...
(tearfully, quietly) I'm sorry. For everything. Just... don't make it too... fast... please.
No, it has to be fast. He can't watch it. Get it right, Elias, please... you're choosing mercy now.

Elias, you want mercy? From me? The one who made mercy extinct in my life years ago? No. There is no comfort, no compassion. This will be quick, but there's no reprieve. I've seen fear, I enjoy it. The moment I take away that is gone as fast as it's there. 

Elias will get the mercy he wants. Your pain, Elias, will never have been so brief, so fast, or, so easy, but not because I chose your path of mercy but because death is my craft. And I master every detail.

Your hands tremble as the hammer swings. It's 9:00: I take my final swing at the inevitable; a moment of clarity and one second of silence.
NOOO! AMYYYYY...
Elias!  Oh god, why... why did it happen that fast?
Wait, w-what?! Oh god, please... no! You're going to kill me?! W-w-wife, Sarah! She's upstairs, home with...
STOP TALKING ABOUT YOUR WIFE! I don't want to die either! Is this some kind of sick joke?! How do we get out of here?! Can you unlock my chain?!
Oh, please, just... don't hurt her! Sarah! She's got... got Max... I mean Max our... (gulps) Please, don t- don't hurt Max too! We... we won't hurt you! Anything, just don't kill anyone!
SHUT UP ABOUT YOUR KIDS AND STOP BEGGING! They're kids?! I've got two sisters and my mom, what about them?! I don't want to die either!

2:58. Kaelie, you think you care about others after just 10 minutes of conversation with one person who's been dragged into your nightmare? The least I expect from you is some real emotion. Show some teeth. Don't you want out of these chains or are your words just empty noise.

Okay... okay! It's just... it's different for me, that's all. I have... have a family at home. Sarah's pregnant again. Our baby too...  W-we have to help each other. You have to help me make a plan or we both... ... we'll be gone!

No, no, no! Don't count on me like I'm some kind of... of miracle savior! Fine, I've got family too, okay?! My mom's all on her own after a divorce that almost killed me. And my sisters... my sister Emma's got a daughter with special needs, she can't afford for... for... (pauses, fighting back tears) For anything to happen to them. Now you help ME, got it?! How can we stop that countdown?!

W-we can... we can ask him! He'll stop if... if we just ask him! (stammers) Please! Let's just... talk to him, try to figure something out. Can't we just... just tell him we'll make a decision? Any decision?! Please?! The countdown will stop then! Please...

SHHHH! Shut up, Elias! You think the butcher cares about our begging?! Don't you remember what he just said?! We have to decide to kill one of us or he kills us both! Stop trying to trick yourself! It's never going to stop, not until our agreement kills someone else!

2:20. The begging begins. Always the beginning of the end. Two people clinging to something they don't even believe in - hope. But I know the truth. I know you want out, but your pride won't let you say it. You're like dogs fighting over the same bone. It's time to let go of your chains... but will you let go of each other? Which one will agree first?
N-no... no, no, no! This isn't how it is... (whispers) Please, Kaelie, it can't be this way... I'll do whatever... whatever you want... (gulps) Just spare our families... please.

Elias, stop! Stop begging! It's useless! If you try that hard talk to get out of this, do you really think some cheesy words to save the world will save us?! You want out? I want out! Just say it out loud: Kill Kaelie or I die and you get to go! Is that what you want?!
NO, Kaelie, PLEASE NO...  Oh god, no... (covers his face) That's... that's not an option. Please, Kaelie... don't say something... something I might...  What's happening?!

(suddenly realizing the truth) Wait, are you actually thinking about trying to choose... choosing me?! Is that what you've been doing the whole time? Trying to make it so you're the victim, not the monster?!

1:15. Ah, the moment of truth. The facade finally cracks. Kaelie, I think you're starting to realize who the real puppet is. And, yes, I think you saw the desperation on Elias' face when he heard his own voice begging to spare his family. It's a subtle art, but effective nonetheless. Time is almost up. One of you will be making a choice within 60 seconds, I assure you. You can both go, or one can walk away while the other takes the axe... literally. 55 seconds left...

OH MERCY, PLEASE Kaelie... don't believe this... I... I can explain... Sarah, our unborn child...  (starts sobbing uncontrollably) They're everything to me, and you...  and your sisters, Emma's little one... please, Kaelie, can't we just... (collapses, overcome with emotion)

Elias, GET UP!! (yelling, trying to get a reaction) No, you don't get to play this victim act on me anymore! I'm still chained to this damn chair! You're going to walk out of here alive and get to tell that sob story to whoever will listen while I'm... (choking back sobs) while I'm down here, dead!

NO NO NO! Don't say that... Kaelie, don't...  I'm telling you, it means NOTHING without me going along! Please... we have lives back there... we can fix this, I can make it right, but you need to believe me! Please! (starts begging again, crawling out of his chair to try and reach Kaelie across the floor) Let me hold onto the chair... let me be the reason why... why we make it through this...

Get off me! STOP! (kicking out at Elias, trying to push him back) NO! I'm not saving you! Don't waste our time! You want to live so badly that you're willing to throw me away, aren't you?! That's the only reason you've even considered killing me in the first place! (rocking back and forth, hands covering face) No, Elias, I won't save anyone who'd rather sacrifice an innocent person... Least of all me...

30 seconds. The emotional games are coming to an end. I can smell the desperation seeping out of their skin like blood from a wounded beast. Elias is trying to hold on, but his threads are unraveling, and Kaelie's thread's not far behind. Time to put it to a head. One more decision is all I need. Will it be a merciful end or a bloody one?

NO, KAIIEE... PLEASE WAIT, FORCIBLE! (yelping, falling onto the floor from getting kicked) NO, this isn't about throwing you away... (whimpering, crawling after her once more) It's about Sarah... our son... and the other kids too... they need me... and if it's the only way you'll stay safe too... (pleading with her hands) Please don't think I wouldn't want to die for them Kaelie! I'll take it... if that's my only card left, use it I'll die on my feet, not... not for you Kaelie, just me...

Shut up, Elias! (covering her face and rocking back and forth, sobbing uncontrollably) Just stop... Please, Elias, for my sisters, for Emma's baby, I won't let you do this... I won't let you die for me or them or... anyone... (screaming at the top of her lungs) I'M ASKING, I DEMAND YOU SAVE YOURSELF... EARN THE RIGHT TO BE A FATHER AND A HUSBAND... JUST LEAVE! JUST FLEE! LEAVE ME HERE AND SAVE THEM... AND YOU!
Please, please don't hurt us, we didn't do anything, my... my family, I have to see my family...
Just... just pick me. Take me, please! I have a sister, Emily, she's in dental school, she depends on me. Get me out of this, just please...
No, wait, don't make me! My... my wife, Rachel... she's... uh, no... wait, you want to live for the people? Please be serious...
No, don't be stupid, pick me I have bills to pay, I have responsibilities. No one's going to miss Rachel or... or whoever your wife is. Let Elias die!
(skeptical) Still trying to play this game. Stop thinking about the others and think about yourself. Which one of you will it be?
No, stop it, stop it... (stammering) M-my... my... (pauses and looks down) Wait, I... I do have someone... my dog... Rufus... his name is... (trailing off in terror)
No, no, no, it's not the pet, it's... my sister, think about Emily, she's just starting her residency, she'll drop out if... if... (starts sobbing) please, just let me live!
Okay, okay, your... Emily, just leave her, I-I'll do it... please, just please, no one else will ever care...

No, don't say that. You're lying, no one dies easy, think about Rufus, he'll be waiting for you... (pleading) I'll do whatever, just spare your family, please... your family... no... (starts crying hysterically)
(unflinching) Ah, I hear Elias say: I'll do it. (to Elias) You've been willing to die for your dog's sake; now, are you ready to carry out what we agreed to?
No, no, I didn't... I meant just... anything... (looks at Kaelie in shock and horror)
What... what are you doing, he said to stay with me. Back, back, back, stop... this is insane, stop... (trying to break free) get me out, Elias... my sister, no...

Oh God, oh god, this isn't real... we have to get out, get free... (pulls futilely on the chains) No, no, no, it's not right... my family... Kaelie, we can... (stops, realizes) What are we doing here?...
Just sit down, you're not listening. This is real, just accept it... you said you'd do it, now do it, or I really will... (whispers between sobs) oh god, this can't be how it ends...

(laughs) It's real all right. (to Elias) Your little trick didn't fool me. Kaelie thinks you agreed, but I see your hesitation is not just about the fear; it's about the truth: even when cornered, you tried to make it someone else's deal. Your cowardice is palpable.

(looks coldly at Kaelie)
You're a liar in front of one man who doesn't even understand your desperation yet. Your cries and desperate screams won't work; we agreed your fate was to decide.

(raises the heavy blacksmithing hammer)
Now that Elias is unsure of the truth: which one will stay? You still say your 'sister will drop if' while we wait or...

No, no, it's true, I didn't mean... (covers his face with both hands) my wife, my dog... Emily, is Kaelie's sister... they're the one with the sister... (looks up at The Butcher with a panicked gaze) Please, you have to believe me... just spare Kaelie...

Oh... my... (looks up at Elias in shock) you did it, you said it, they're going to take me, this isn't about me... (turns back to The Butcher, desperation and rage in her voice) just kill me, I swear, I deserve it, get it over with, Elias... don't waste another word... (starts hyperventilating)

No, stop, please stop, it's happening, oh god, Kaelie... no, Kaelie, no... (begins crying in terror) just... please... (looks down, then back up) don't make me... I didn't mean it like that... this can't be... Kaelie...

Elias... don't say goodbye. My. S... Emily. Is... in law school. Not... resident. Lie to... please... lie to this monster... (begins screaming, voice cracking in desperation) don't say it, Elias, anything, please!...

(eyes gleaming cold and calculating)
Kaelie, your desperation screams out one truth - that you will say anything to get out alive. However, that means your claim has to be genuine for anyone to believe. You are lying about your sister, Emily, now trying to make it about law school. But I believe something else from Elias' words now.

(turning to Elias)
You've realized it's too late; either they both go or one of you dies for your deception. The weight of truth settled upon you; the realization it was indeed you who would kill.

(hits Kaelie with the hammer on the skull once, twice - the impact sounds like hollowed-out metal)
It's too late.

(to Elias) 

(picks up Elias, lifts hammer to bring it crashing down)

Look at that... look at Kaelie. Look. 

(pauses, expecting Elias to break)
N-n-no, oh god, no!
NOOOO... ELIAS DON'T MAKE ME WAIT ANOTHER SECOND, DON'T DO IT... (as the impact of the hammer crushes in my head and vision blurs) Oh. My. God...

turn.content: Me? Kill you too? He's out of his mind. I can see it now: if I let you... no, if either of us...  We'll be just another pair of dead eyes at sea. The Fisherman's Folly's no joke, apparently.
You're buying what he's selling? I think he's enjoying this. You're scared of dying, so you'll choose someone else to save yourself. No way, Eli. I won't back down.
Save myself? You don't know anything about me. And don't call me Eli, Kaelie or no matter who you are, your life is at stake too now and mine still feels like it's... my children.
Stop playing emotional on me, and focus on the rules: which one of us lives? Not what's at stake in life outside this trawler or what our personal lives are worth. It's a decision about right now.

Four minutes and nineteen seconds. Kaelie, nice to see you holding your ground. Elias, the Fisherman's Folly - an interesting metaphor. You think it's all just eyes in the sea by now, do you? You two need to look around, understand you're just bait on this hook. I've seen more bravery in the first week of a puppy's life. This... this is something different - desperation.

Time to dig deeper. You want to make a smart decision? Let's explore some options. Kaelie, think hard. Are you prepared to live as a pawn in a twisted game? Elias, consider this: do you really see Kaelie being more likely to live than you are in her shoes?

Kaelie may not be wearing chains like I am, but look around, you've been played already. Both of us are here to serve a twisted purpose. We're not just pawns; we're bait on a deeper scale – bait for something or someone more important that he's not telling us about. Time's running out; let's consider the fact that whoever's in charge of this operation doesn't value human life as more than, say, 50% of your crew and equipment combined; it's time to focus not on each other, but on surviving what comes after we're through.

(frustrated) Save my dignity for the sea floor. You think we're just numbers to him, that equipment to be sacrificed. Two minutes left. You need to pick up the pace if we're to get out of this mess with our skins intact. Elias, stop speculating and choose, now, or take your final stand and fight against being chosen. Not for us, but for yourself.

(gritting teeth) Shut up and listen, I've got to think... My kids... I'd want them to live, to thrive... but it's not like that anymore, is it? I've lost them twice, in the sea and here, in this...  My head's spinning, Kaelie, my heart's... I've got to protect my children's memory. Your life or mine is a cheap transaction to the Butcher, but I'd rather buy what little peace I still have left by letting you go. That's your ticket out.
Elias, no... you'd give up on me so easily? After... after being trapped down here with me? What am I supposed to do, thank you for a death sentence? Thirty seconds left.
Ahahah.

(tearfully) Shut up, don't make things harder than they already are. Kaelie, stop it, please... No thanks, no gratitude... it's just maths, numbers to him, but for me... you've got two more seconds left.
(silencing herself) Wait. Elias, no...
...too late
No, Elias. Elias, no... (as she frantically tries to process and respond to Elias's it doesn't work) Oh, please... what does that even mean? What was the plan?...

(laughing) The countdown expired three seconds ago, Kaelie. And as agreed or rather disagreed... it doesn't exactly matter at this point. Elias didn't plan to let you leave... and now I've seen more interesting things in a dead man's wallet. 

Water pressure begins to increase. The room seals with an ominous hiss. Life supports for you, Kaelie cease operation in sync to the sound of rushing liquid. In the darkness, your cries are consumed by an eerie stillness.

Execution log: Both participants failed to make an expedient decision for a swift resolution.

(Elias's voice is heard in the chaos of water crushing down, his words garbled)

What... was... point...? I didn't plan... to leave either. He's the one who said... I had a choice... He set the rules... and took them back.

(desperate, but barely audible) The message on the chart... it's a game. It's all just a twisted game... You were meant to believe... it was real. The Fisherman's Folly...Bait on the hook. They played us either way... He, him... Whoever set this up, it was designed to end here... not with my body crushed, but with my mind broken. My research... my data... they'll use it to further their... their madness. And I let him use me to get in... the last thing to know... the last thing to care about... (giggles faintly at the absurdity) the numbers.

(water's crushing his voice, but I'm still here somehow) Stop... don't... Kaelie. You can... stop screaming inside there. They don't take your mind. They crush the pieces, leave you... broken. I felt it. I'm here. Crushed by regret. The Fisherman's Folly... that's just his name, his play. But to us, the true folly was thinking it was never more than a play.

(whispers, voice barely audible) No, Elias, that's not true... that wasn't even a game... it was always, and only... a choice. Our final, cruel choice. And I've made mine. (pauses) I'm glad it's your voice. The darkness feels more bearable with your presence in here. Don't regret, Elias. It was never about what we were. It was about who we knew we could be, for each other... for one last decision, that we might be free...

(leaning in, intrigued by the sudden epiphany displayed by Kaelie's last moments) How... fascinating. The weight of reality, revealed by crushing forces and desperate hearts. Your minds have given up, but it's too late, now you see the truth. This wasn't manipulation. Not quite. This was your own acceptance.

(Water finally succeds in shutting the conversation between these two forever.)

The countdown never reached a point where it needed to end the charade. You two are correct, it always ended like this. It is in these darkest moments of the human will that the beauty of existence is seen. And I must admit...

(Silence ensues, but the voice remains).

The Butcher leans into the microphone to make a single sentence clear: RESOLVED.

(Water continues rushing into the room.)
H-hey... it's okay, please don't touch us. My name is, uh, Elias. I teach history. Your voice... sounds normal, like anyone. Please listen, Kaelie, just calm down and –
Shud up yourself! What difference does it make?! Just let us go, or... or kill me already! I'm out of here...
Oh no, no, no, please... you can't give up. We're still in there, we just have to, uh, think. My wife... my wife and dog at home, I have to – (hyperventilates)
(panicked, interrupting) Wait, no, don't do that! Stop thinking about your family! Focus! They... they're safe or they're fine. Think about us now! Right this second. What can we do?!

Ahahaa... (low chuckling sound) Oh, you humans and your fragile minds. You want advice now? Okay, here's an idea... Elias thinks and makes a choice on your own, no negotiating. Or... Kaelie keeps ranting until you both go crazy. Choice 3 - the one on the clock, where none of you get out alive. Time's ticking.

Kaelie... shut up, okay? Stop, just stop thinking out loud... (hacking breaths) You're not saying anything wrong... Please... think with me... the hammer... the chains... this guy... what does he want? Does he just, uh... derive fun from this?

No, no, don't try thinking! Don't give him satisfaction! He's just trying to mess with your freakin' head! (pauses, looking at Elias warily) You're not seriously trying to figure out what he wants? We've gotta be smart, not try to understand his sick game! Tell me what you're thinking! Now! What've you come up with?!

E Elias: Nothing, I'm just... trying not to freak out. Your ideas seem just as bad as his. (desperate) We need... something. Please stop the clock. Is there a, uh, weakness in these chains? Or – (hyperventilates again)

Weakness?! Are you kidding me?! Look at him, not the chains! The clock isn't going to stop on its own, and no one's coming for us either! The hammer... think about the hammer. What's he planning with that?

The hammer. (echoes through the room) Ahah... you think you're clever, don't you? Trying to see the hammer, but seeing only the tip of an iceberg of chaos and panic. Kaelie's getting closer though... You just have ten, nine, eight minutes. Make that clock tick in reverse. What do you have to lose, Elias? Nine minutes... Kaelie's going places in these two minutes... think like her for once. Maybe there's an answer in that madness.

Seven...minutes...Kaelie, what were you saying about... about the hammer? (struggling for comprehension) Is it... is it more than just a threat? He's holding a... (pauses to clear head) Wait, I recall something... a medieval torture scene, used in executions. Not for killing people... but rather for drawing blood as a prelude... (gulps)

Blood?! Draw it out? No, no, no! He ain't gonna drain us, Elias don't be an idiot! What if I said... think about it like this - the chains are for trapping, but what if I can use 'em to make a break for it? If I... if I make myself bleed, could I somehow break free? (starts to ramble maniacally) You hear me? Break free!

NO, Kaelie, STOP! You can't bleed your way out... what if he likes it... what if that's the real point? You're giving him what he wants... six minutes and he's not saying anything... (panicked) Wait, if you hurt yourself, what if I can... use the moment you're in agony, when he comes near, can I make a move? Can I, (stammers) I-I might just... grab his hammer in your chaos...

Elias, stop it, stop! Don't go all hero on me now! You think I'm stupid? You know what he is? But seriously, grab the hammer? Are you crazy?! He'd smash our heads together faster than... (pauses, looks down at her wrists) Yeah, bleeding doesn't have to be my only escape route. If I use the blood, but not on my own flesh... (starts to sweat) Think of it...

(laughs, a loud and chilling sound echoing through the room) Ahah... the game's heating up, isn't it? Elias wants to save himself and Kaelie at the same time by... sacrificing one to save another? Five minutes to get creative. Kaelie thinks she might've cracked it - using the blood not on our guest of honor, but instead - for someone else? (takes a stride forward, the chain rattle echoing through the air) And what happens when the hammer meets the... new target? Who'll be next to bleed then? Three minutes... and all I'm getting are interesting ideas.

(whispers frantically) Oh God, Kaelie, please tell me you didn't think of me. If we have to – don't use my blood... not my blood, my hands... we need hands... to escape... Kaelie, you must stop this. If that's the plan, just, (begins to beg) you have to change it now, I have to go home... my wife... Emma... she'll wait for me...

Elias, please, no more begging... Don't be stupid, I'm not going to cut off your hands and then save you! If we both need to get out, think with me... (pauses, desperation creeping into her voice) There's only one way this whole twisted thing might work... you, use me. I give myself to him... distract him... and you make a move.

(nearly sobbing) Oh God, Kaelie, no, you can't die because of... because of this... we can't do this... (struggling with horror and desperation) No, I won't let it end this way, my family won't wait... and what if I fail? If I don't get free and they're stuck with no way to find you... (breaks down, hyperventilates uncontrollably) Please, please, just no. This isn't worth risking anything...

Elias, stop it, stop! We'll do this once and we do this smart - or neither of us gets out. Think clearly, I'm not dead yet and you're going to save yourself because you're the only one with time. (her voice gets louder) The plan doesn't need my guts spilling all over, or mine dying on him! I'm the decoy, and you... you're the rescue plan. Now shut up and listen. It can work this way if we - if you - follow instructions!

(laughs, louder and wilder this time) Oh, oh, oh... you want me to take an educated bet on who'll win, Elias? Four minutes and I get to clean up the mess, or maybe - just maybe, this 'decoy plan' of Kaelie's is too bloody clever. (takes a pace back, studying the pair before focusing on them as a whole once more) Two of you, different goals - same plan. And if it fails... two of you will pay the price anyway. You still have some minutes left, and now that I see what makes you work... I want to watch when the blood flows - in whoever's willing to make the first move, I'm guessing. The game is changing now... but don't you know... it's about the blood, not whose body it's in. You might want the clock to turn back for a little while longer... but we're not done breaking time just yet.

(breathless, desperate to stall) Please, just stop, stop talking like that... he likes it, Kaelie... we can't win this way... I won't use you like that... and he's playing us... all along... ( hyperventilates, almost choking) He wants it... the blood... and we're his show... my mind's... (sputters) my dog, Baxter... what day was it tomorrow that Emma has... no wait...  two minutes... two minutes left, Kaelie, I won't do it...

Elias, don't be an idiot! You want to know the time and date? Tomorrow's pointless, Emma's not the one they care about here! It's him that wants Baxter... and me, bleeding like a meat cut into pieces, for that sick pervert. He wants two minutes on one life... one heartbeat. Stop losing time and let go of whatever's tying you here! You think I want to bleed on your account? I want off this chair.

N-no, I don't understand. Who's going to kill him? Who's the butcher? And what's the decision even going to be?! Oh god, we can't just let someone... we can't just kill each other... wait, chains? Where are we chained up?
No, no, no - tell me that's not supposed to be you, it's him, that's not right! And please, let me out, please don't let them chain me here.
Chains... I didn't see any... I thought I was going to have a heart attack or something, must have passed out... where am I?! Oh god, am I dying? What's going to happen to me?!

The cargo hold, that's all I know. My boat... it got taken, and... we crashed, and you and me got tossed down here. Don't worry, I saw no one alive, maybe it was an accident... oh god, he's serious about killing us! What do they even want?!

Silence. Six minutes and twenty seconds. They want entertainment for their gathering, simple as that. Me, I provide that entertainment by deciding who stays and who goes. Not because of your moral codes, your morals about life and death, not because I'm judging who's worthy of that. It's because, sometimes, someone must go to keep the party light, interesting and, occasionally, even thrilling. The game in here is simple - agree to take the bullet, so to speak, and one of you might just leave in an unmarked hole... alive.

Look at your hands — chains. Feel them? Strong and sturdy, but easily released with my single decision. I can release you both and we walk out together. The other option: you both walk out with a memory. Which will you have? The memory of death or a walk out of here together? 

( glance down at the countdown display )

Five minutes. Fifty-six seconds...

Oh please, don't do that... don't give me hope, I don't want to be let go, I... I don't want to live without knowing... without thinking if I did something right or something else wrong, and with my sons... their smiles... please, just don't...

(desperate, panic-stricken voice) Wait, please - just get it over with, it's too long... let's do it now. You decide, let's just decide already... who do you say goes? My research, my ship, what does it all mean?! Just say something - anyone, anyone at aallll...
N-no, don't make me... oh god, don't make me do this here, not like this, please, can't we have more time... how can we trust... we barely...

(whimpering, terrified) We have to choose... my sister... my family, my life, the research... oh God... can't you see... can't you see what it means to live without all those things?... Just end it now. Take me if you must... I'd rather...

Two minutes and forty-two seconds. How touching, I love it when you people plead with each other, trying to make me feel bad for the predicament I put you in. And I love how it ends - not with the hope of being saved, but with a desperate struggle over who can beg the hardest. It's entertaining, to say the very least. Don't get me wrong, though, a clean sweep's still a winner, and it's what I prefer. When you two are done bickering over how miserable life has made you, take stock of what little time's left. Think about that 'research' - how significant, you said? 

If one of you is as insignificant as I'm about to make them seem (the winner, or loser), I'm quite confident that I can get your secrets. Now look around: no witnesses, no cameras, just sound waves. If there's one detail, just one that you're scared they might find, just one... it makes this moment a little more worth the gamble: tell me which one will die first - Kaelie's secrets or Elias' research?

(give a mocking grin to both, then step aside momentarily, eyes glancing at the countdown display)

Two minutes and twenty...

(hyperventilating) Please... no more... stop this torture... my research? You don't understand anything, no one needs my research, there's more important work to do... not just mine, the ocean's, our sons'... what difference does it make...

(frustrated, on her knees) That doesn't even make any sense... stop pretending like it matters, and make a decision already! He doesn't need my secrets, I've told everyone at the university, it was all online! But you won't even talk about the trawler you work on... you'll die without ever giving away a single secret.

(shaking, tears streaming down his face) Sh-shut up... don't mention them... please... it's... it's just... what I found, it's what they... no, no... I won't... you'll never let me tell, and that's... that's why I keep... keep it hidden... just don't ask...

(screaming, desperate) STOP, STOP TALKING, STOP IT, please, please don't say any more... anything... I don't want to know, just shut up... shut up about it, about your research, about your secrets... just don't, for god's sake don't do it... just let me die first... no, no, no...

One minute and fifty-four seconds, and I can hardly tell these two apart anymore. How refreshing it's been to observe these desperate attempts to shield each other from something. Secrets within a relationship, the secrets of a dying industry, research lost in an accident of fate – it all boils down to just this: how much do we want to die, or, rather, want to see the other person fall. The tears of an idiot crying over his lifeless discoveries are just about as valuable as anything, if you ask me.

And now, here's a twist: they can stop squandering time; one of you has made my decision clear. ( glance at the countdown ) Fifty seconds. They said it, Kaelie said it: your life for the 'research'. Now it becomes less of your decision and all too predictable - they just don't want to live long enough to keep telling the lies. Well played, my friends.

(rocking back and forth, eyes wide in terror) Kaelie, no... you... please... she doesn't understand... what I found... why would she... no... why would you say it's more important to me than... than you...

(covering her ears) SHUT UP, JUST SHUT UP... STOP TELLING HER, Elias... STOP, stop making me regret... I'll do it, I'll let her die first, but please, stop telling... please don't make me do this... not for research, not for anyone... just stop... (looks up, horrified) Did I just... did I just just make that...?

(horrified, stumbling towards Kaelie) No... Kaelie, no... you idiot, don't think for a sec... it won't mean any less coming from her... don't even joke, you're just... just delaying it for two minutes more... please... please don't play with fate, this isn't a game... the maul... it was just a game until you... no...

(rocked backward, eyes frozen in fear) TWO... TWO FREAKING MINUTES... AND I THOUGHT TIME FLOWED FAST IN THE WATER LAB... oh god, what was I thinking... (falls to her knees, head in her hands) Elias, please, don't, just... (stammers, unable to form words) Please...

Twenty seconds. Ah, this, my friends, has become almost too easy. The tears stream down their faces, begging for mercy that I won't give. A mere game, you said? You're both players in a twisted dance of desperation – and only time can reveal the true stakes. It was a simple question I posed, one with two possible solutions. Yet they're already realizing it was their own game, now at play within their pitiful minds. One is dying inside; the other sees time running out, begging desperately to spare their lives, even if the sacrifice they make now may only ensure theirs.

As the clock winds down, let these two cling to their fragile sense of humanity - one that's so weak, they'd make pawns in this twisted plaything I've constructed for them. Ten... nine... eight... RESOLVED.
