turn.content: Okay, okay... stop shouting. It'll only end bad if you don't calm down. Kael... listen to me, we can talk about this and find a way out... but we gotta focus, for both our sakes.  You, there... with the maul.  We'll figure something out, okay?

Focus? Our lives hang on this and you want me to focus? There has to be another way - what if we tell them you're crazy and they set us free? But they don't know what's at stake here - if the wrong person leaves, I don't stand a chance...

Don't say that. Don't remind me... of what happened. But yeah, we gotta be smart, not reckless. There's got to be another play, another way. Think for a sec, what's in it for this guy? Something tells me we can use that to our advantage, get a little extra time, or find a crack in his plan...
Extra time... but even two minutes won't be long enough to escape if we can't figure out the layout and the crew's routines... I need a chance to think, think about anything but this...

Five minutes forty-five seconds. I see. You think there's a way out in it for me? A reason I wouldn't stick to the plan? Tell me. Make your argument. You see, I get a bonus for meeting a clean and silent execution. But if there's even a hint of blood shed outside these walls or the slightest sound beyond my ears, they take that bonus and take a part of something I value. I have reason to listen. What is it that you propose that'll save our... little charade the agony of a clock winding down?

It's not a guarantee, and it comes with a cost, but I saw something on the navigation chart... 'The Fisherman's Folly'. This isn't just some old crewman's joke. It's a code, a clue. Whoever set this up, they wanted us to think it's just a random message, but it can get us out of here. It has to work... because if the clock hits zero and my sons'... they'd want me to keep moving. For us, for Kaelie... we don't make anyone richer by dying here, quietly.

Your sons? That's your leverage, not a reason for us to be killed. But what if we do manage to break free, or get attention from the authorities? Are you certain your... bonus comes before family life? There has to be a line somewhere...

Kae... I swear to you, it's like they say. Family means everything. My sons were always my reason, but they... they're gone now. And in this crazy, cruel world? If anything, being trapped makes me wonder what their last thought was, what their final moment felt like...  But yeah, that thing on my back, the pain you see, it was them too. That's not what keeps my head on my shoulders, and it's not what you gotta worry about now. The price for breaking the system is... too close to breaking me, Kae.

Your sons... that's all you have left, and you're willing to trade that for my life and who knows how many others you'll end up killing as part of this twisted arrangement. I don't know what's more twisted, the situation or the way you keep mentioning them like it's proof of your decency - decency you're willing to sacrifice to hold onto life itself.

One minute, twenty-five seconds to a decision. Kaelie, you mistake this for some emotional display, don't you? It's just the weight of regret and pain – the perfect counterweight to desperation. You should appreciate it – that desperation will drive you into a corner just like I've driven these two.

Kael, I told my boys... I promised it would be okay if they fell overboard one day. I'd never be here, like this. No one would even look for us... and that's what I was running from, from when it really hit me that it was true; my boys might have still been alive and I was just waiting – or I'd rather say hoping. That makes it harder for you, does it?
No, that makes it worse for me... that a single, shattered promise might save our lives. You traded a life for a death and a lie, and now my death will be the repayment.
Silence now won't save her, And that's what I told my boys. A lie won't save anyone – not in death or the darkness that comes after...

You've had time, Elias... all this talk, the broken promises, the lies... and yet, silence is still your strategy? Even in death? That only tells me I'm still worth nothing but a desperate gamble. And as for the boys, maybe I'll find them for you, in the darkness, to keep the conversation going.

Thirty seconds, and your conversation becomes nothing more than a distant noise, a mere distraction from the inevitable. This sentimental back-and-forth doesn't sway me, nor will it stay my knife. I have the power to end this at any moment – your futile attempt to find reason or humanity in what I've become is as pointless as a desperate whisper to deaf ears.

(Every time you say a line as another character, The Butcher interrupts, with a timer that continues to count downwards; in this scenario, the time left is 22 seconds).
Kae, don't be bitter, even now; at the last second, I'd still call her a friend for trying to reach what's left in me... (glance up)
At least I'm still worth being referred to as 'a friend', not just a number in your little wager. Even in my death, you get to claim some shred of morality, don't you? (glares at the Butcher)

Time's almost up, isn't it? Fifteen seconds. Just like that moment when everything went to shit with the boys - in and out, the waves took them, and that was it. This time, it's just Kae; she says 'a number in your wager', but you know what this feels like to her right now?

You know exactly what it feels like - being discarded, like so many other statistics in your life. A mother whose boys drowned on your watch, now facing her own death in the same cruel indifference, a number to be tallied, not a life to be saved.

Ten seconds. You dance around each other in desperation, like moths fluttering near an extinguished flame. Elias, you know this will pass to Kaelie soon – you've calculated the moment precisely. And Kaelie, I wonder if you comprehend what drives us: it has nothing to do with you being discarded or saved, but in your role as my leverage for control. I'll give you one chance to break this stalemate:

(Turns to Elias with raised axe)

Choose how I die first. Decide who goes first.

(eyes closed, face contorted) Stop it... this isn't a game to play. The timer and your axe, they're just echoes of my nightmares. My boys were in my hands; no matter how I turn it up, no matter how fast it spins, I still see those waves carrying them away. Kae, I swear, I didn't choose for you to die here.

You can't choose to decide? You're as invested in me being discarded as The Butcher is. At least he's being upfront with us; it's sickening to see how far into his hell I'm dragged before recognizing it, with his leash through you.
My... wife... oh no.  We can't do that. Please, Kaelie, don't listen to him. He's crazy! We... we just need to wait for someone to find us...

Don't play dumb, Eli. You think I'm just gonna sit here while that thing counts down and watch you get it? No way. You'd really do that for your precious little wife, huh? She's not even here! What do we owe him, anyway? Why are we on death row?

No! That's not what I meant. I just... I just wanted to help you make him stop. We don't deserve this, Kaelie. Please... (starts hyperventilating) Please, just don't say anything else. He's... he's going to hurt us. (coughs, trying to calm down)
Shut up with the tears... it's gonna get worse, don't you think? This is the end. We gotta play his game. Think. What can we tell him that he'll believe?

Your sentimental crap is almost touching, Elias. Don't get too attached; your tears are already seeping onto that floor where you'll be meeting your end.

(looking at Kaelie) Your turn, little lady. What have you got to offer?
Wait, no... stop. Kaelie, don't do it. Can't we... can't we tell him the truth? We were just, uh, students... or, I was your teacher... We're completely innocent. (stammering) Just please...

No, Eli, no! That's exactly what makes no sense. We're students? Teachers? What, you got a fake ID I don't remember? No details. Just... just keep him here for a minute, thinking about the game. Let him see if we're as desperate to choose as he thinks.

(gulps, trying to stay calm) Wait, just... wait. Okay. Let's... let's stall. Tell him... tell him we need to think because it's too hard or something. We just can't decide that quickly. (tries to stall for time) Is that okay? Can I try? Pleeease just let me talk... (stammers, eyes locked on the clock ticking closer)
Just do it, Eli. Tell him I'm... overwhelmed. That I need... yeah, a minute. Something. Anything to buy us some time, don't waste it!

The Butcher (skeptical): Oh, this should be good... Elias, you really want to try and play the 'we're scared because we're so innocent' card, huh? Well, that ship has sailed.

But, (suddenly interested) if this 'thinking' thing will make it more... entertaining – I'm game until that 5-minute mark, let's see. I'll give you sixty seconds, Kaelie. (taps the counter) When it hits one, you've got your minute. And don't think about trying anything fancy.

Clock still moving...

(nearly in hysterics) Okay, okay! Just a minute. Please, just... think Kaelie's got the minute. (whimpers) Please... don't do it, Kaelie. Don't even think about doing whatever he says if Kaelie decides on me... please... (stares wildly at Kaelie, begging with his eyes)

One minute, one minute, sixty seconds. Focus, Eli, or so help me... don't freak out, get me through this. I see something... think... (motions with her head) what was I saying? Think, Kaelie... no, what are we doing? Wait for it, wait for the minute. I've got a plan – don't move. Just stay here and stay quiet. (staring at Elias with a frantic intensity)
(terrified) Okay, okay... shh!  What's your plan?

(whispering) Quiet! Just 12... seconds. Keep... the clock in sync. I'm going to play along, say something. See if he's predictable. I'm gonna talk to myself for a minute. Play a trick. Let's see... (glares at The Butcher)

(leaning in, intrigued) Ah, now we're getting somewhere, little Kaelie. You think playing dumb might just be our ticket into... a deeper level of entertainment?

(smirking) I love this, I really do. You're either clever, or desperately attempting to survive. Either way, it's too early to decide. So, let's get on with these 40 seconds, shall we? Try to entertain me with some of your clever tricks, Kaelie. Make sure that little heart stays racing in your chest, and keep those clever ideas rolling in. The more it burns, the better I'll enjoy. The Butcher leans back, arms crossed, waiting for Kaelie to continue her little routine.

(breathing fast) Kaelie, no... don't get his hopes up. We can't let him think he's got the upper hand. (stammering, nearly choking on his words) We can still run... or... or... (realizes his chains are too long to move) Oh no... Kaelie, I...

(whispers urgently) Shh, Eli, silence! Act scared, but play along... start a conversation – ask him a question, keep it going. I'll keep talking to myself, maybe create some plausible story, while he's listening to yours. (starts speaking in a panicked hush) Why not ask, what's with the clock?...

(stammering) The, uh, the... the clock? Is... is it, uh, some kind of... countdown? To... to... for something to happen? (trails off) Does he have a game in mind? Is it, uh, going to hurt us? (seeks Kaelie's gaze, hoping she might understand) What's going through his head, Kaelie?

(speaking rapidly, eyes darting between Elias and The Butcher) Yes, yes! Exactly! You hit it! The countdown... a game... (pauses, pretending to consider) what if... he's just trying to lull us into a false sense of security? (voice rises, attempting to appear uncertain) what if the real game is just starting, and he's... waiting for... that... 5-minute mark... Oh no, what if it's not a person we're choosing... but something else...

(amused by the conversation) Ah, Kaelie, you're a creative thinker. Your desperation is almost palpable, like an air you can almost smell. (pauses, letting the words hang in the air) The countdown. The false sense of security... yes... (chuckles to himself) You see, it's not just a game; it's a ritual. A ritual of choice, a ritual of... consequences.

(leaning forward once more) And as for what lies beyond that 5-minute mark... well... (leaning in, a mischievous glint in his eye) let's just say I've been saving something special for the two of you. Something you've been quietly wondering since you found yourselves... in this... position. Elias, your heart rate tells me you know exactly what I'm hinting at... and Kaelie, don't you think it'd be wonderful to find out now?

The clock is now mere seconds away from running out, and Elias has 11 seconds left of this 60 seconds minute... time is almost running out.

(hysterical) No, please... don't... it's you, isn't it? It's... my family. What's it about... what have I done? (looks wildly at The Butcher, begging for mercy) My wife and dog are all I've got... please... don't touch them!

(interrupting, voice rising, desperation creeping in) NO, ELLAS, STOP, STOP! (looks at The Butcher) You're a pervert, isn't he, a pervert? That's who he is! You have something sick, something twisted, and you're waiting for some twisted pleasure at our expense. (sees The Butcher's smug expression) Oh, God. That's right, isn't it?
Wh-what... what's going on?! You say we have to decide? Which one of us... Oh god, please, let's get out of here, we can figure something out. Please...
Wait, no, don't even think... let's not... Elias, listen to me...
W-what, what's stopping you from... from making the decision yourself? Why are we the ones choosing?!
You're stupid, aren't you? You really think we're the ones making the decision here? The one with a maul, telling us to do this? No way, this is your little twisted game...

That's right, your game's afoot. Kaelie has a point, though it might not be in the way she thinks - this is, in fact, one of my games. I enjoy playing the odds, observing as pawns like you try to make your decisions, often based on desperation and emotional impulses. Don't flail like fish, focus: Five minutes and ten seconds, and you've still got nothing. What'll it be? Five minutes left to ponder...

(As you pause, a faint, sad music softly plays in the background.)

What do you want from me?! You already got your sick pleasure watching, observing... stop the music... this isn't fair, this isn't... ... Oh god, the song... the one I used to sing to the boys...  Ahh...

Shut up, just shut up... and stop playing with us... Elias, calm down, don't give away anything... what do you do for a living, huh? I was a marine biologist, tracking the fish population in this area...

Fish... population? You studied fish? I used to take my sons fishing... out there (points at the hull) on my boat... we'd catch fish by hand, and... and the little bastard I used to take them on, his name was Sam, the old fisherman...
What's with the old fisherman? What's he to you? What happened... with Sam? Please, tell me... stop the ticking! Anything, just...

The Butcher (interrupting Kaelie): You want me to make it easy for you now, don't you, Kaelie? Elias has a weakness with you. Three minutes and five seconds - a small time, but the stakes could be your eternal rest... or, on occasion, a life in prison. Don't tell me, Elias. Tell Kaelie. Tell your precious, the marine biologist that has her attention. Three minutes left, two people to make a choice... what'll it be? (The song starts fading in the background once more as your emotional conversations begin making my work easier)

Oh god, it's... (pauses, struggling to form words) My boys... they drowned. On my boat, my kids, my boys... we went out too fast, didn't take the right precautions... Sam, my crew, they were trying to get the engine going, but it was my boat, my fault... (shakes head) My boys just... didn't make it...

(stunned, tears streaming down her face) Oh no... your boys... Elias, I'm so sorry... this isn't your fault, I'm not... (composing herself) Wait, no, stop it, focus, think! You killed someone, Sam... the old fisherman, the one you trusted... didn't you? Did you sink your own boat on purpose? (anger rising) Tell me, was this some kind of revenge?

Re... revenge? N-no, not like that... I just... lost focus, I wasn't paying attention to... to things on the engine, on the propeller... it wasn't what you're thinking, it was an accident, just a dumb mistake... (looks down, a deep shame on his face) You don't understand, those weren't just any people... my children, and Sam... I should've looked better, been more careful... my god...

(backing away, terrified) Don't come near me, with that axe... don't think this means anything, because my life can't make up for your kids... but I want to live, I want to... tell my sister about my sister... not about your pain, or your guilt... please get the time, how long we have left!

Two minutes and eleven seconds. A lot is at play here, isn't it? Your memories, Elias. Memories of the past, memories of loss, of a tragic accident, but also guilt and blame. One and a half minutes for your thoughts, one decision for your fate... and Kaelie's too, if you play it well. The question, of course, remains how far guilt and despair drive humans into destructive impulses. Do I want justice? No, I want a performance. One and fifteen seconds to choose... Kaelie has her eyes closed, is she willing to trust you? Open them and decide before time runs out...

Don't look at me like that, she's scared of me, she thinks I'll... I won't stop. (glances at Kaelie, who's eyes closed) No, she needs to see what I want to... I want to be sorry. Sorry for everything. My children, Sam, for even being here. Kaelie, just... please.

(screaming, still shaking): No, don't say that! You can't just wish your pain away! Open your eyes, Elias, stop staring! And what do you mean you're sorry? For sinking your boat? You didn't mean your kids, they were accidents... you mean for this? For The Butcher?

(panicked) No, stop reading me mind, stop, just listen. Just hear me. (takes a deep breath) I'm sorry for all the things I wish I'd done better... for Sam... for the life before, and after. Not for what The Butcher does, no... just for all the what-ifs that keep me... stuck like this.

(eyes snapping open, desperation creeping in) Stuck?! Stuck on your feelings, your pain, your guilt?! That's no excuse for... this! (glances at the timer, only 47 seconds left) What's going to happen when it ends?! Will you kill me too, or just... release us?! Tell me now, before time... (trails off, pleading look on her face) please...

(grinning) Your pleading is wasted breath at this stage, Kaelie. 44 seconds... the end is in sight. A deal like this can't leave anyone unbound from their fates. (Eyes on Elias) It would be so easy for him, and for me, to give into what is required of them: blood, guilt, the past - the only thing left between these hearts. We have reached an agreement, without us saying it. RESOLVED.

turn.content: Wait, chains? What are these chains?!  My boys... do you know what happened to my boys...
Boys? What are you talking about?! What boys?! Get away from me, I'm the one being held here! You think I'd let you in on some secret with me being trapped in this crate?!
No, god... I'm sorry... it's just... we were on my boat... and a wreck happened... Emily and Timmy are... are they with me here too? Do you...

Emily? Timmy? Where're they?! No... no way, I don't see any others down here... Please, if you're trying to distract me, you're doing a great job, but I'm the one being held hostage! Focus on me and get me out, not your... your kids!

Your little boat wreck... how... touching. As for Emily and Timmy, that's your good news, folks. You might see them a lot sooner than you think. Or... rather... not think. (chuckles menacingly) Let's focus on your current predicament. That clock's still ticking. You've gotta choose who's first, Elias. Get on it or watch the both of you suffer.

Notices and whispers into his wrist radio:  

Takes a closer look at Elias' confused expression: Time's almost up...
No wait, what... what did he mean by that? My boys... they're... dead... and you're saying?

Dead? Who told you that? And even if they are, doesn't that have anything to do with you and your... (snaps) Wait, focus, Elias. They don't need you or your sons right now. This boat's the issue, not you and your grief. What can we do to get out of here?!
Get out of here... no one told me... I saw my boy's smile, every night in my dreams... and now... Kae... help me, think what I need to know... the chart on the wall...
Think?! Help you think?! That chart... The Fisherman's Folly... What's that mean?! Is this thing... is this the wreck you talked about?! What's going on, Elias?!

Ah, the chart... the Fisherman's Folly... (chuckles to himself) Yes... That was the boat's name, my friends. I've been keeping it in mind. (looks at Elias' anguished face) You thought you'd left your personal demons at the bottom of that wreck, but nope... Now they're right here with me. (tosses Elias a piece of the torn fishing map) You might've thought you were lucky to not get yourself lost, Elias. But you see... sometimes, even those who get off the boat can take the wreck within them. (stares Kaelie straight) Kae can tell you what I mean when you decide, but time's still up for you both... The Fisherman's Folly has given you what you asked for. You see, when people talk to the spirits on your boat... it starts to get stuck in their minds. Your boat is a tomb in more ways that... you know about.
(horrific realization) What's happening to me... I can smell the oil and... and my sons... They were always on my boat... Oh god, I killed them myself somehow... (whimpers) What am I doing?!

Stop, stop talking, stop thinking... You're making this worse, Elias! You're falling apart and it's giving us nothing! That... that man's messing with your mind! We can't afford it! We need to get out, now! What can I see around here? Tools, anything to help us?!

(snaps into a hasty analysis, eyes fixed on the controls and equipment) Safety rope, a hook... it's over by the crate we were just in. I saw it when...(gulps) I was trying to see through my boys' eyes... Maybe if we use it, we could... I'll show you, Kae, just stay with me...

Stop, stop touching me! Don't let him get to you, Elias! You're not listening! Focus on that rope and hook, not whatever darkness is eating at you! Get it, now, I need something to work with... and stay with me, no wandering off... (points at The Butcher) You might have an agenda with that, but I have one, too. I want out of here.

(grinning menacingly) How touching. Now you're focusing, Elias. But not on getting out, no, that's just Kaelie trying to survive until you give my the answers I'm after. Your little epiphany, however... it's priceless. You're getting a whiff at the truth, and now that's what has me... (leans in closer) interested.

(Walks calmly towards Elias)

The Fisherman's Folly and its secrets are beginning to unfurl within your psyche, and it looks like the wreckage has awakened something you didn't think was possible. (picks up a small tape recorder from one of the boat's lockers, places it within earshot) You see... sometimes, your darkest thoughts can reveal their true forms when they're captured like these two... on tape. What Kaelie calls the truth. You've been walking these memories, thinking the pain was all behind you. But no one escapes the wreck. You can choose to keep playing along... but you can't deny me anymore...

(backing away, trying to maintain a fragile grip on reality) He's manipulating me, Kae, this tape... he can use it how... I won't let him see... (whispers, a chilling thought) The boys... on the recorder... They're saying something... I won't believe it...

Shut up, Elias, for the love of all that's sane! Don't listen to it! What's he trying to trick you into, a confession? Tell me you're not buying what he's selling! Get your hands on the rope and hook, now! We break the lock on the container and get out, that's all that matters! Not the voices, not the visions, we survive!

The voices! The voices are my boys! (clutching at his head) Oh god, they're trying to tell me something! (looks at Kaelie with pleading eyes) Help me believe you, Kae, help me believe it's just a trick... Please, it's all a test... But... but then I can see them. (sternly, grasping for a semblance of control) I remember the wreck. The Fisherman's Folly crashed against the rocks. What happened that night? How can we get out? I need the rope...

Yes, yes, focus on the rope, Elias... (grasping your arm, pulling you towards the crate) We'll worry about the rest later! I need you to pull out that rope and we get to a place to... wait, stop, don't say anything more! You can't be thinking about this right now, Elias! Just the rope, nothing else! (pats the crate) Where is it?! I know it was right here... (yanks open drawers and containers) We'll find the goddamn rope and I'll help you survive this...

(watching Elias crumble, eyes lit up by excitement) The truth... the voices... your boys... they want out as badly as you do, it seems. You are a man torn apart, and I... get to collect your broken pieces. (laughs low, a chilling tone) It doesn't matter how we get out of here, nor what you believe. You've seen the wreck, relived it, and that's all I needed. Your little epiphany just confirmed the truth I've known all along... (takes a step closer to Kaelie, now grasping the rope and hook) Elias, you've been trying to outrun the wreckage, but it's been inside all along, guiding your hands towards me. Heed the voices, listen to the spirits on the Fisherman's Folly, for they're finally speaking... (tends his hands to the lock of an old crate, takes it in two steps) Time for the last ride...

(stares, frozen, as The Butcher moves towards the lock) No... no last ride... My sons... they don't want any part of this. (struggles to regain control) Kaelie, no, don't let him. Whatever truth he's claiming, I'll deny it. (gives The Butcher a resolute glare) He's got nothing but my imagination to blame, not real voices...

Shh, Elias... just be calm... we're almost... (glancing at The Butcher, a silent plea to delay) Wait, let it be what it is, we need to play along just long enough... (to The Butcher) But at what cost? If it's just a broken mind of ours... what do you even want with us now?
No, no, no, no, please! We have to talk, we... we have to think! What kind of monster... Oh god, my family! They... they rely on me...
You think I don't have people I love too?! Leave me, him, whoever... Just let us alive! Do something sane, stop this, whatever kind of psycho you are!
Okay, okay! Just calm down, I'm trying... I'm trying to think... What's the... what's your name, uh... what do they called this...  Oh god, I need to...
Don't waste breath, focus! My name's Kaelie. Don't you dare try to sweet-talk your way out of dying...

Your boyfriend and potential escape attempt. Good luck with that, Kaelie. My name's not up for discussion either. Focus. Time's wasting: decide who gets to live while the other dies. No escape talk, only decision.
Kaelie... your... your friend, family... someone... Do you have... someone back home too?
Yes, I have family... I have siblings... Don't try to make me feel sorry for the two of us... We both end up here, or you'll choose, and you'll be the monster!
No, no, no! It's not that... I want to live... I want to go home... My wife... her name is Karen... and our Jack, our little dog... I need them...
Shut up... don't do this... you're choosing me maybe? Just pick the right option, please, please, please... just get the chains off...

The chains you want off. That's simple for me. Not for you. You're weighing your lives against each other, with a few sentimental attachments on your side. Now the choice: pick yourself, and the other will beg as I watch; you can try to pick the other to save, and maybe, just maybe, you survive – and see your wife as a distant, fading memory; or, finally, try to trick me into thinking you're working together while secretly planning your escape... and we'll both know you never stand a chance against me. The timer reads six minutes. Choose, already.
N-n-no trick... I... my word... I choose you, Kaelie...
Why... what are you doing?! You fool, I won't even have a chance to thank you... You're making a deal with my killer... This is it, isn't it?! He gets off, free!
P-pledging yours... it doesn't have to mean that I don't... that she leaves you all... she... she can still go free...
Stop... lying... It's not true... You're not thinking of Karen, you're just trying to give me something... anything to hold onto right now...

(Chuckles menacingly) Ah, the human condition: hope, guilt, desperation. Your little emotional exchange has bought you nothing but more time, Elias. Kaelie remains a prisoner – of the moment, for now at least. However, your words have created a... twist. I see a potential story unfolding here – the wife. She might yet come to join the conversation, and that raises interesting possibilities. For now, I remain intrigued by you, Elias. The story's not entirely over just yet. You may still find a way out, but it's my turn. I take Kaelie to a small side room... You, however, now think on one thing: What will happen to you when I bring your precious wife into our negotiation?

(Breathing heavily) No... please... don't... I won't be used as bait... My wife, Karen, she won't... (stops, composing himself) Wait, you have her, don't you? Where is she? What are you going to do to her?
(From the other room) Stop! Don't! Tell him you took us home! That's it, tell him! Please, Elias don't give in! It'll be worse for your Karen...

(Straining against the chains) No, Kaelie! Don't try to protect Karen... (to The Butcher) Tell them the truth! We have a dog, Jack... a small house on Oak Street... we have a white Subaru... (hyperventilating)

(From the other room, terrified) Elias no! Don't you make it worse! He'll know exactly where to find us! We'll... we'll disappear together! Don't give him that! (Silence for a moment) Wait, what's that look on his face?

The look? You mean fascination? You want to know my inner workings? Elias's desperation and Kaelie's... caution. Your attempts to navigate the moral labyrinth. The way you people make your situations so much worse when you try to make them better. (Pauses and leans in, speaking softer) Now the truth. I do know about your little family. Jack, your canine companion, and your lovely home. You see, Karen is already here, and you're running out of time. She's in a separate chamber, just like this other dear... You have exactly 5 minutes and change to reach an agreement that would spare at least one of you and Jack, if Karen stays quiet... but let's keep playing games. The door behind Kaelie... the one that has a small inscription on it... the name? It says: 'For the mother.'

(Tension in his voice) M-mother... (Stammers, comprehension dawning) Karen... our dog's name is Jack, Kaelie said, I told him... no, not your mother, the dog... (Realization hits, and a wave of horror washes over him) 'For the mother' of my baby son...

What... you... you think Jack's still here? That you... my own kid's in that side room with... (Gulps) I won't trade for Karen! Or for Jack! Take me, I'm the one who deserves it! I was the one working! (Pounds chest with chained wrists) Kill me! Me! Not him, not our child!
Wait, stop... wait, please don't kill me. Kaelie, listen, we have to talk, we have to –  Ah, my wife... my family...
No, no, no, stop stalling, you idiot! Look at the guy, he's not exactly subtle. Focus on staying alive for now, then we can think about our friendship later.
Oh god... she's right... what's wrong with me?! Look, just... can we work together or... I don't know someone we can trust to report all this when...
Report it? Who do we trust?! There's a guy wielding a hammer, and water's seeping all over this place! You want to call someone over a phone?! Look at us, we're bloody bait!

(chuckling)  Aww, you think it's funny when the people I catch beg each other for safety? That's the best part. Alright, Elias wants to know, can they work together to survive? Sure. That's exactly what I want to hear. Tell me, Elias, do you trust... her, over there?
N-no, okay... okay, no, I mean... Kaelie, I... you need to... we both need to... maybe not trust each other, but we have to survive this together, or he kills both of us!
Fine. Let's be crystal clear, Elias - I didn't ask for your help, I'll do what I need to. But, for now, we need to pretend the alternative is me getting my face rearranged.
Okay, just... just don't talk like that... think about my family, please. Think about Sarah, we just talked about her recipe last night...
Save the soap opera for your executioner, okay? We'll think about Sarah, we'll think about your whole family... after we're out of here.

(laughing) Ahahahaha, you two are so quick to bond! Elias, begging for a way out with sentimental attachments, and Kaelie... using her usual brand of icy realism. This conversation is almost as much fun as their eventual demise, which is just on schedule to happen. 6 minutes left.
6 minutes... no, that can't be right, time is moving so fast. Okay, focus, focus... must find a way out... Sarah, please let me make it through this, just one more day...
Snap out of it, Elias! Time isn't moving fast, it's just going to be too long. We're stuck here with him. Focus on survival, not your precious Sarah, your dinner recipe!
Okay... okay, right... survive. Think with your hands, like math, patterns, think... is there something here, something to use against him, something hidden...
Chains, we can feel them, he wants us thinking big, not little. What if those metal links aren't just for show? Maybe we can... no, it's too big for us, you'll break them.

(whistling a happy tune)  Oh, poor little Elias is thinking about breaking free already! And your clever Kaelie wants to use the chains as a makeshift... whatever. You both think you're geniuses, but you're just playing into my hands! Let me show you what those chains do when you try to manipulate them...  (with a menacing grin, begins unscrewing Kaelie's wrist restraint with a screwdriver hidden behind the hammer in his other hand.)
NO, WAIT! Not that, anything but that! Kaelie, you have to - (struggling against his own restraints, making desperate gasping sounds as he chokes back on anxiety)
Back! The other way! Make him work it loose, you moron! (grunting as metal digs into wrists)
OH GOD, Kaelie, the bone! It's going to...! Don't break, just please-!
NOT SUPPOSED TO BURST THE WRI-   OH, YOU'RE...  AID!

Ahahahaha, priceless! Aids the weak, indeed! (as the chains finally come undone and Kaelie slumps to the floor from the shock) Well done, Kaelie, I'll give you points for ingenuity in death! It seems you've freed the wrong wrists in my little game. Now, poor Elias, you can watch in agony as your last moments are spent screaming, knowing that your girlfriend's pain comes at the hands... (chuckles, then raises his foot to strike) well, at my... not so gentle touch!
KAE-LIE, NO... PLEASE... GET UP... PLEASE!  Don't let him kick you... Oh, God... this can't be... I have to make it stop, I have to... think, think, THINK, SOMETHING ANYTHING! MUST STOP HIM!

MOVE AWAY... Elias, GET YOUR BACK TO HIM NOW... Don't... can't... you idiot... (grunting and straining, managing to prop herself against the chair and start a slow, stumbling crawl as the killer lowers his foot, momentarily surprised by her unexpected action)

turn.content: Wait, no one has to die. What do you want from us? Why are we locked down? My boys... I need to think... what have you done to my boys...
Shh, shh, it's okay, let me think. We need to get your boys first, but... but you. How do I even... What do I do? Tell me what you want! What's going on?
Stop... stop crying, child. Think like me. Think like a fisherman. What do the Butcher... and you get from making someone decide who dies? What's happening out there?!

They get... they get to watch you kill each other. It's a game, but I think it's about something else. The Butcher is after something, and he's using me for it, but my research vessel was just... what if it sank him, something big?

(sneering) Ah, now we're getting somewhere. You two think you can piece together your own little murder play while trying to outsmart the Butcher. You've got that part right. It's not about the killing. It's about the fear. Fear creates power. Power creates the show for those watching, including your precious Kaelie. The vessel did go down; and I got what I wanted. But the real game begins once you decide. Don't waste anymore time, though - that clock isn't going to take long to tick away. You know how long? Long enough... to drown you both... and make the real game go live for millions out there.
No, no... millions? You can't... you can't broadcast this! What is this... what's happening?! They're showing this?! My boys' memories...

Memories? No, you don't understand. That's why I went on the dive, the data, I was trying to protect that... our research, it could have changed everything. And you know, he... the Butcher knows too well, it involves people like him and their 'game of power', but the vessel, I can see parts... on a live stream, a live stream that millions can see! Our lab data's going live, it...
Your research... could change what?! You can't think about that now, what's going through my head?! Our boys... you think that means your research would've kept them safe?

No, Elias, I think the reverse! If my findings surface, it'll take them down! People like the Butcher won't risk exposure - it's what killed my ship in the first place. I knew you fished in this area, I thought you were clean, that you didn't know the extent... but something made them come after us. This is bigger than a fishing trawler sinking!

(smirking, leaning against the cage that keeps Kaelie in a tight spot with just an edge of metal on her body) Oh, the more you two figure this mess out, the more thrilling it is... And Kaelie's got pieces to play with here: you want answers, Elias, I've got one for you about your research – I've got all about your research vessel and the dive they did down. It's quite fascinating, what you all were looking for down there...

(pacing across, he pulls back and leans against Elias' cage)

Elias thought his boys might die if it leaked, but the truth is: they'll find themselves more likely in danger because... your children could expose me if your research makes headlines.

(leaning in for effect)
They could end the show before the new season even starts... and that'd be a kill... shot. For everyone involved. (He pulls his mask over to reveal a maniacal grin for only Kaelie and Elias.)
No... they'll use my boys as leverage? They'll harm their own crew, their people... you'll kill them to keep your secrets, just to keep on with your sick game, won't you?!

Stop... Elias, he's telling you this to make you see, to make you pick someone to die, to keep the game going... don't believe it! The ones in danger aren't your sons, but our research – and with it our findings could take them down. Your crew is the real target... the Butcher's been playing you this entire time. He's already destroyed my ship for your own secrets.
No, no, you're lying! My boys are already dead, they can't even protect your research now! You're doing the same as the Butcher, playing me like a fiddle... stop it, child!

No, Elias, please... listen. I know what happened to your sons, I saw it when the ship went down... they drowned because of those very secrets we were trying to uncover. Don't turn on me, I'm trying to get out of this alive, same as you... but the real question...  ...is, which one of us walks out?

(laughs maniacally), Ahahahah, see, Eli... You two are starting to play it just right. Kaelie's saying something that might just make you choose to save your own... by losing someone else, and not just any someone... You're being a perfect puppet, dancing to the strings of this deadly game we all play. (chuckles) Don't waste your energy hating me, Elias. Hate the reality. Kaelie's right; there are only two of you now, but... one of you walks out... (leaning into Kaelie's cage) which one, hmm? Which one's about ready to give up their fate?

(Turning back to Elias')
But you're not thinking of just any two - I told you, you'd know, Kaelie's words... she said she thinks she knows what happened to our sons, doesn't she, Elias? So that raises only one choice for you - now that there's a way out - do you pick one or take on the one chance of escape together? Think like a fisherman; how are his options, hmm?

(grasping hold of the bars, eyes wide open in panic) No more choices, stop twisting everything! You're saying they drowned because of the secrets? That means they died for no reason, just like you're playing me now... No, I'm not choosing... (voice rises to shout) I'M NOT CHOOSING EITHER OF US TO DIE FOR YOU!

Oh, God... Elias, wait, stop fighting it. He's telling you your fate is tied to this, to what you think you can control, or the risk of losing everything. Don't shut us both down, we have nothing if both our options are gone, right?...

(defeated) Shut us both down... that's what happens when the Fisherman's Folly gets its due... (eyes drop) But that chart, it says folly... a trick? What were you even researching, Kaelie?! Was it supposed to be a warning for the ones who fish here?!

(struggling with the realization) No, no, that's the thing... it wasn't, not exactly a warning, but... the patterns, the shifts in the data... I think it's... a warning of an entire system failing... The waters I've been studying... I realized there's no way they can keep up this demand, this exploitation... It can't last. That's what the Fisherman's Folly message means... the price of your livelihood... your lives is our ignorance... of this catastrophe looming ahead...

(leaning in even closer, so his mask is within a centimeter of Kaelie and Elias' cages) Ahahahaha... Catastrophe?! You're talking like some fishy philosopher... (he grins) And what makes you think, oh wise one, that your so-called message can save either one of you from me? The Fisherman's Folly has more than just charts and numbers - it's the price people like YOU will pay. Elias knew this when he sent the boys diving too deep, Kaelie knew it's why she's running for her life, because... you see, the system's been crumbling in secret... 

(chuckles)
The ones you love, they can only save themselves... from the ocean's depths, or from their own guilt. Your warnings of a catastrophe won't change a thing when it's done... but they've certainly changed my approach... now it's just about... (suddenly serious, he steps back, and with both a chilling grin and raised tone,) the way they drown. 

(The dim room lights seem to dim with his gaze and he leans in, his whisper almost deafening.) Kaelie, Elias, the only real secret you discovered wasn't the fish that would save them all, but their fate, when the real ocean closes over their heads.

(eyes locking onto the Butcher's, a mix of defiance and despair) Enough. Enough games. You're going to make me drown in these secrets because you think you're the Fisherman, the one calling us ashore? ( voice rising in anguish) You killed my boys and now I'm supposed to join you, dance for their memories and accept I sent them to die... You took everything from them, and now you're going to take their memory and replace it with your sick story.

Shh... Elias, just stay down, the more they see us fighting, the better it is for them, or rather, him; they've got the upper hand. We're just bits of the larger story... he's taking every secret from us because he wants to keep the show running, that's all. I know what this is: a performance, and you playing your part will give us this catastrophe you mentioned, but for you and your crew, in your own twisted terms. Just give him the performance he's looking for; he'll do the rest. Please just...
N-no, please, stop this, let us... please.  My w-wife, back home...
Just kill you and get it over with. Nobody asks our permission in this mess. What do you care about Elias's wife?! What have you done?!
Oh no, this can't end like this I'll never see... Luna, she's got that new... new recipe for chili. She... oh, I...
Recipes... shut up about recipes! What's going to happen to you... after? When the police come? What are you going to tell them about us? What are you going to tell them about...

Save the pleading, Elias. Don't waste my time. But Kaelie, you got me curious with that. What the hell do I care about what I'm going to tell the cops? This way, when they find you, Elias and his lovely Luna can just... never see that precious chili. I'll show you your 'lovable life' after. You've got one of two answers for this, though. Elias's wife, your life, your 'recipe' for what happens next - which one of these does it?

No, Kaelie, no, don't say anything, just be quiet... what does he mean he's going to show us anything...  

P-p-please, just let me go, I'll never... tell Luna, I swear, just don't do anything to... please have mercy, it's me, just me...
Elias shut the FUCK up! Think about anything, but don't think too hard, okay, your wife... what's her life gotta be compared to mine?!
K-Kaelie, no... don't say stuff like that, don't... it has to be me, K-Kaelie, please, just... Luna loves the sunset we take every weekend...
Shaddap with the sunsets, okay?! What do I care about your pretty pictures?! But... fine. It's him, just get it over with. Let his stupid sunset views rot with his wife.

Ahahahaha. It's him, huh? The sunset-loving guy who cared enough for the woman at home, not you Kaelie. Nice. You see, Kaelie, Elias knew his sunset and Luna loved it, that's the key difference right there. Now, for your benefit, I'd like you to say it, clear your throat and tell me: Let Him Go.

(stammering) Oh no, please K-Kaelie don't do this... say what... (he tries to breathe deeply, hyperventilating) I-I love it, yeah, I love my sunset and I'll take my sunset home... just, just let me go and I swear, Luna'll keep her pictures...
Let... HIM... go.
(exhaling heavily, tears in his eyes) Thank God, thank... I won't ever...
SHUT UP, Elias, just leave and GET OUT!

Ahahahah, oh, perfect timing. Kaelie, it seems, isn't even sad. Elias... on the other hand, has given me a newfound appreciation for those pretty views you care so much about. Kaelie, now it's time to find out: Do you stay and let Luna keep taking pictures of... those precious views with Elias, or do you live a life with me?
(backing down, eyes frozen in horror) Stay... with me, stay with... stay with me instead...
Stay, of course, I stay, just leave... don't touch me, please... (she chokes back a sob) Please don't touch me.
(nearly catatonic, whispering in his chest, over hyperventilating) Luna's gonna see Max, Max's gonna be okay... the sunset... the clock is going down...
(terrified whisper) Oh God, oh God, oh God... MAX?! What about him?!

Haahahahaha, it's too late for that. Max won't be okay, sweetie. You see, Max will never know another sunset again, thanks to Elias here and your... interesting choice. Elias knew about Max. He knew about your child. It's you and yours against you being with the man whose views you despise. Now that you've chosen Elias, and it seems you have, tell me, Kaelie, how are Luna... the sunsets...  and now Max... just going to work?

(hyperventilating, words stumbling) Oh my g-god, they need me... they need Max... no, they're all I have now... Luna needs, Luna needs, she can't cope... without... (he breaks into sobs, voice shaking uncontrollably)
(screaming) NO, NO, PLEASE, DON'T HURT THEM! WE'LL DO ANYTHING, JUST DON'T HURT THEM, PLEASE!
Wait... I... uh... please just... I'll do anything! No, no killing! My family... I have... my wife and... Baxter, my dog! Please, you don't want to...

Shut up, him! Focus on getting me out of here, you idiot! This is all your fault, isn't it?! Don't look at me, you pathetic wimp! We have to talk, Elias - who made you their plaything? What were you thinking, coming after him?
What... wh-what? No! This isn't me... I was just a teacher, a history teacher... I was just taking a shortcut home from school... and then... and then... ... oh god...

Spare me the sob story! I have my own life, my own job... Dental assistant. I didn't volunteer to play your psycho friend's game. What do you know about how I got here? Were you even taken, or is this some twisted trick?!

Ha. You two are like puppies trying to chase a ball. Kaelie, you're right about this. You didn't choose the location, but both of you, at this point, had a lot more going on than the 'what.' Elias, you said 'the path' you'd be taking wasn't what. You said 'was supposed' before... the event... of your life took a different route. So, Kaelie's getting closer. You know some truth...

A different... different route... that's it... no, it can't be... oh lord, the meeting with Emma... she was new in school, had trouble with...  What if it's about Emma? The little Emma that died under my care?  What is this about...
Stop, don't say another word! You don't get it! The more you say, the deeper in hole you dig. If this is about Emma, and you're somehow involved... how do I get out of here?

Please... no, I'm innocent... I didn't hurt anyone! Don't believe The Butcher... he's crazy... it wasn't me... Emma was so sweet... and my wife loved working with her... Baxter... they would go on walks... together, you know? Please, help...

Stop trying to play victim, Elias! Your lies are suffocating. I don't care about your stupid walks, Emma, or your innocent routine. Look at us now – chained, threatened, and on the mercy of this psycho. I have nothing to lose but my sanity and maybe my limbs.

Time's wasting. You two keep staling, your odds of survival decrease every passing minute. Elias, Kaelie thinks you're trying to fool her. She thinks The Butcher is just any lunatic. But then, there's your hesitation on it, and Kaelie knows she can only save herself. Now, make your choice before I turn off the lights, and your darkness lasts forever. You have 5 minutes left. Which one goes, Elias... and is it by your choice?

Oh god... it's... it's so simple... ... K-Kaelie... you have a future... family, life... please, for your daughter's sake... don't say... my name... just don't say my name, I beg you...  I'm going to lose... my mind... Baxter, be gentle, come here...

NO, NO, NO, ELIAS! You're not even getting this right! I've got nothing to do with a child, just shut up! We need an answer, who goes? You're the one connected to Emma, maybe Emma's death isn't as dead as you think...

Oh god, stop... stop it... Kaelie, please... don't say Emma... it was... it was an accident... I swear I tried... I kept her safe... Baxter's safe with Sarah... please, for Sarah's love... please don't let it end... like this...

ENOUGH! You're really taking away my last thread of sanity... Tell me what you know so I can maybe...just maybe... survive this nightmare! You're dragging me with you down to the depths – tell me about Emma, and let me get out of here... PLEASE!

(laughs, his deep voice resounded in the eerie silence) You both want an end, one way or another. Well, that's exactly what I'll give you. Kaelie demands truth, but is willing to lie, if it means saving skin. You are, after all, the last person standing when you said... your future 'family life.' What kind of life is waiting when your past comes back... Elias, you know I am always a reminder that sometimes 'past mistakes' have 'fatal repercussions.'

It's decision time. Your clock just expired, with no time for last goodbyes or apologies...
My family... Sarah... Baxter... they'll never have the chance to see me in chains... in the ground...  No, Kaelie... I won't let it end like this... but it seems it's... out of my hands...

Don't...don't do this! Say something, tell me a lie, I care! Don't go there in front of me! Wait, it can't be the daughter, is it... you said 'family', I said 'child', did I unknowingly confirm that Emma's accident was yours...

Kaelie, please... you can see, you have to, it can't be a daughter I'm talking of... but it... it hurts like one... a family I love so badly, a love I... my love for them's a lie; Emma was the first; first love, Emma... the clock expired too far, you know what this means, you see the truth, Kaelie... in your eyes, my eyes... oh no...

GET AWAY! STOP SAYING HER NAME! Emma's a lie, an obsession, a connection, and you... you... you've killed Emma and... your family... this is all connected to you! How deep does it go, right? You've been digging the dirt, burying secrets, while I was stuck with Emma in the clinic, making smiles and cleaning after people like you...

(steps forward, a maniacal grin spreads across his masked face) The truth's finally revealed. Emma wasn't the only one with a tragic connection to Elias' existence. I'll let the truth seep in before my verdict on who lives. But here's a thought that will drive Kaelie into a madness that would outdo The Butcher's cruelty.

Kaelie's eyes widened at a realization too profound for The Butcher - a secret buried deep enough for the world to forget, the kind no one sees coming.

Kaelie... my... Baxter... she doesn't know, I made sure... no, she doesn't remember what I told her... that's... what makes her safe... no one else knows... you're not thinking, Kaelie... the past was supposed to be just that - the past. My past is... Kaelie... is my child

NO... THIS CAN'T BE... WHAT?! BAXTER... IT'S ME, ISN'T IT?! YOU CAN'T BE THE FATHER OF BAXTER, I COULDN'T BE THE CHILD OF A SERIAL KILLER! WAIT, IT'S IMPOSSIBLE... YOU'RE THE KID I ASSIST, THE CASE FILE I SAW... YOUR STORY, IT'S ALL... MY STORY, IT'S ALL A REPEAT...
