turn.content: Wait, stop, don't do this. I just want to get out of here. Please, just... what are these chains? How did I get like this? My kids... think about something else, man. What are your chances, yeah, your chances, let your kid be.

Get your mind off my kid, yours or whoever! Look, this isn't some kind of game, I think we were on a... a research vessel. It got destroyed, I think. Fisherman's Folly... it was a ship. We have to keep talking, he's going to get a thrill out of this, let me think.

Research vessel? You think that's this boat? Oh God, if we were on that... I knew someone. I fished nearby, and... I saw something burning, but... too far out, it was too far for me to... and then it was too late. Kaelie, did... did we get off that ship? Before... before it sank?

No, we couldn't have gotten off. The ship, it was... (pauses) destroyed, it went underwater. I was trying to... get some readings on the school, the fish, they were getting so low in numbers... and then... (trails off) Wait, did you see me? Were you part of...? No time for that now!

(chuckling to himself) Ahahahaha... You still think you can solve a mystery instead of deciding who lives and who dies? I like that about you. You've almost used two minutes and haven't decided on a sacrifice yet... that's either genius or morbid curiosity. I'll give you three questions, each one will cost a different amount from your two minutes to spare. You ask 'em, Elias... will this help ease your guilt, choosing who lives or dies? One last thought: what if your friend Kaelie has no one to visit while they... rest?

(The sound of a timer ticking fills the room.)

(panicked) No, no questions! Not yet! We'll think... we'll figure something out, together. Don't do this. I just... I want my kids back. What if they're still out there, waiting for... for me to get home? Please, don't do it. Give us more time! 

(The desperation starts rising in my voice) 

Can we, can we call for help? Send a message or something?! Is that even possible here?!

(nearly shouting over the timer noise) Stop, just focus! Elias, think about a rescue! Can he even hear us? I'm pretty sure there was a radio on the ship... but it went down too. Must've been damaged in the sinking! (seizes his arms) Listen to me! We have to use our time wisely, if... even we make it out of here! Who were you fishing with? Did you see or hear anything else that night?

(clinging to any hope) Rachel, Rachel was with me... she, uh... she came on board later that night... my assistant, my wife... no, my wife... (stares off for a moment, reliving the pain) No, please, think... after Rachel, when I came back... I saw... another trawler, it was near... (looks at Kaelie desperately) You're sure there's no chance... no chance we can get word out?

(clenching her teeth) I'm telling you, we don't have a signal to send! And if we're stuck under there, no one's ever coming for us! (pauses as a new thought strikes her) Wait... Elias, that trawler you saw... was it... (hesitates as panic grips her) The same crew, The Butcher?... Could one of them have...?

(laughs, his voice echoing menacingly in the room) Ahahahah... Oh, Kaelie, you want to go down that road? Elias thinks he's got some precious time to play detective, now his precious memories start unraveling. (stays quiet for 15 seconds before speaking again) Time's still ticking, my friends. But, just for kicks, yes, it was one of our crew members, that 'Trawler' they've been fishing nearby for, but you won't find any of their faces. We... changed them. When Rachel, your lovely assistant, came to the vessel, you were both... guests, of the Butcher and the Boys.

(As I pause for comedic effect) Now, think about these 'girls at night': they got to enjoy a taste of that vessel, a taste of The Boys' party. Your memories have gotten so confused it seems, that there's something fishy going through those minds...

(screaming silently) NO, NO, NO! Not Rachel, please don't... she was trying to save my kids, my poor sons... they were all I... my life was falling apart... (he's on the verge of a full-blown panic attack) Get her out of this, make this stop! We were all just supposed to fish! How is this possible?!

(shouting over his anguish) Elias, stop! Your memories are wrong, I think we were both just witnesses to something. We have to survive this, think this through. Think about your children, they need you! What were you even told before you were chained up here? Did you have a choice or...? We need to focus on the one thing that can possibly get us out of this. (looks around frantically) Do you see that chart on the wall? Maybe there's something, any clue about how to...

(broken) Choice? There's no choice. You're telling a story, Kaelie, but I don't know what to believe. ( looks at the chart) The Fisherman's Folly means something to him, but what about us? He said we were on the Folly... but was that before? No answers. My mind is torn in pieces, it's the same nightmare every time. Just get me to my children, let me see... (stares longingly out, his eyes brimming with tears)

(grasping his shoulder) Elias, please don't lose it... We have to find an answer to save us both! Look at that timer... We can only ask for so much more help. What else... what if he's just messing with your memories? Think back, before the boat sank, was anything about us different? Anything that stood out? Anything... (tries to snap us back to focus)

(cackles again) Memory distortion is a lovely side effect, isn't it? Now I'm starting to get interested in these fish-bait minds... Elias was thinking he had 45 seconds before time expired.

Time remaining: 31 seconds. You want to focus, you want more than I'm giving you? Elias is getting anxious for an ending... and a family that may not even exist.

You've got time for two questions, one question each, and then this gets... messy. Choose carefully.

As a hint, the 'Girls at Night' had a name on a chart. The Fisherman's Folly might just help you... but will it help Elias get answers before it's too late?

You have to ask your final two questions. Which ones count?

(gripped by a faint spark of desperation) No, wait! The chart... the name on the chart... what was it?! Tell me... tell me what's left on that chart! No one's name, a symbol or something that can be decoded? What do you remember?!

(shouting out her last question) What's on the navigation chart? The chart on the wall, is that MARES INDIOS a name or a port? That's all I got, the rest is... is fading! No time, Elias, our last shot!

(voice fading into desperation) Mare's Indies... (looks up, a glimmer of hope in his eyes) I don't know, it looks... looks like a name, or coordinates... but it's all wrong... I've heard it before... the song Rachel used to sing to me before... she would sing that one song about the Indies, the Mare, when the sea was calm... (gazes into space, his eyes brimming with tears) We had a life once... we had a chance... (the darkness begins to envelop him) Mare's Indies... Mare's Indies...

(frantically screaming at the top of her lungs) RESCUE, ANYBODY! HEAR US! WE KNOW MORE! (realizing it's futile) Elias, don't fade! You're... you're all we've got left... (looks up at The Butcher, rage burning in her eyes) You're not going to break him. No, not on these terms... (the timer goes off with 2 seconds remaining)

(grinning sadistically while clapping his hands, like a father proud of his students) Beautiful, beautiful, BEAUTIFUL performances! And now... it's showtime. Time expired! (laughs maniacally as the darkness closes in) Now, our little dance gets real. Your lives get measured in degrees, and soon it'll be all about how to keep each degree whole.

You'll be meeting the real crew of the Fisherman's Folly... the Mare's Indies is about coordinates, you didn't realize, but that's okay, there's time... more time... You have been part of so many memories... but, I'm afraid they've all led to... the wrong coordinates. (laughs one last time, and the sound is muffled and fading) Well, Kaelie and the poor fool's desperate dreams got him a glimpse... (chained them up and left... as the music starts, a haunting lullaby of the Sea Siren)

You and Elias should probably listen while Kaelie realizes where you all truly are...

(barely able to hold on, his voice strained) No... no, please... don't make this real... the sea... it can't be... my children... are we at the old wreck site? The same one with the sea creatures and nets... I remember it, a night dive with... with Rachel... with Kaelie... and it was supposed to be a routine dive... my routine was my death... was I... was I on that boat with Kaelie the whole time... am I really at the edge of... the Abyss?  The Butcher said we were guests, but this was... this was a fishing ground... we should've...

(she can barely breathe, shock and horror written all over her pale face) Elias... I didn't want to believe it. I didn't want to acknowledge... We're not even on a typical trawling route, it's a designated Area of Marine Biodiversity... the government designated it a Protected Zone... You were telling the truth when you said it felt like the sea was alive. It's as if... it's been... guiding us... towards this, towards... this slaughterhouse. We weren't guests, we were bait. Bait to harvest a different kind of catch. I remember your children... but were they yours or were they... were they part of this, this twisted dream? I don't want to be a participant, I want to be alive. Elias, hold on, we still have a chance if we keep... keep remembering.
Wait, you're... you're going to let me decide who dies?! Oh man, don't say that, don't make me... Kaelie, stop moving, calm down, maybe we can... think this through.
Don't talk to her like she's crazy! I know exactly what's happening, shut up Elias! You're thinking he'll just spare us, aren't you? That's insane, you'll never leave this alive.
Oh no, please, think of my wife, think of... Rufus the... I need to see him soon, I have to get home. Please, please, have mercy. No, wait, Kaelie's right, he won't...
Save the act for someone who doesn't see through you! This isn't some sick movie or twisted joke. You're trying to convince me to kill you, isn't that what's really going on?!

The Butcher chuckles low and menacing, his voice dripping with satisfaction, You're learning fast. Now that I don't think I have to, I can let my mind unwind a little longer with this little dance you two are putting up. You want mercy, Elias? You think she can give it to you? That's sweet, like she'd ever let you live to have that conversation with Rufus. I think what you both fail to see is how quickly your little bond here won't even count. In my house, these chains don't go anywhere. This is all I need to keep it interesting. Keep talking, see if I care which of you I take first... but ten and a half minutes left...
Wait, okay, okay... this is insane... Please, Kaelie, just listen...  Maybe we can figure it out, but please, we need... a way to escape, and a way to survive tonight.

Escape? You think we can just escape? We're chained, this place is crawling with God knows what, and you think I don't remember all the stories about people getting taken from the chair?! Forget about it, we're stuck here! Now we have to talk about who dies?!

Ahh, Kaelie, please... you don't have to decide now. Wait, wait, listen... we can choose... me. Yeah, that's it. Choose me. I'll tell The Butcher yes right now, and he'll spare you. It's the only way, trust me.

Are you out of your mind?! You think I'd just sacrifice you for an out?! You get your wife, your Rufus, whatever, how does that make sense, how are you not even questioning the fact that the killer might just take you and then kill me too because why not?!

(smirking, intrigued) Ah, now we're starting to get somewhere, kids. Elias thinks he can give up himself, and that makes sense in a sick sort of way. Kaelie, you are getting angrier, but it still sounds like Elias has an angle he thinks you can see. I like that. It's not about just anyone dying; it's about strategy here. Let me show you what can happen if we play this game just a little longer. The clock just hit nine and forty-five seconds, give the pair of you another chance to spin some web of lies together. How about we raise the stakes? I'll show you that escape or survival are the wrong thoughts; what needs deciding is how this happens, whether the screaming starts loud and fast, or soft and slow... now it's up to the pair of you...  (leaning forward) tell Kaelie what's real with your offer...

I... I know it sounds crazy, Kaelie, but please... hear me... if I agree to die, The Butcher will give you... an out... a way to live... and he'll make sure you walk out... that's what I get to do for you, my chance to... to be the one... to protect you...

Don't you dare say my name in front of him like that! He's twisted enough as it is! What makes you think he'll give you your way? You really believe he's playing by some sick rulebook?! And what, are you really this desperate to save me?! Why are we still talking about how this all goes?!

Stop, stop... I said I'd die for you... to help you escape... don't... think about it more... (starts to hyperventilate) it's simple, we make the best out of it, that's all... if... if I die, then The Butcher has what he wants, and maybe... just maybe... he leaves one of us alive.

Stop... just stop... (sighs) Fine, alright... you want me living out of twisted altruism? Think about it - what have you got to lose? I already hate being alive right now, your plan might actually work as well as anything else around here... ( Looks at you like she doesn't believe a word) What happens is what happens, Elias... I won. That's all I ever wanted, to not be a victim... for once.

(slightly surprised, intrigued by their twisted negotiations) Well, isn't this a lovely little mess you two have created. The clock just ticked over to 8:59; looks like one of you is running out of time. It's... refreshing to see some willingness to sacrifice on your part, Elias. And Kaelie, while it doesn't matter what you said, your words carry weight with me. Kaelie, you have a particular gift for this twisted game of ours. (pausing for a moment) And, to be fair, I can see one advantage of Elias's 'offer' that's worth playing out. I think there might be some... satisfaction in granting him a 'mercy.' Not much, but a sliver of it will do. Elias wants something he thinks he's earned; fine. For tonight, he gets it... but make no mistake, Kaelie. This only delays the inevitable with me; in the end, you're both going the same way. And remember that, because when my fun is done and you've walked out... it will indeed all come crashing back down. It will never end. You'll always return... but now...  2 Seconds Remaining on 6 minutes...

No... no, it's not that I want mercy... (hyperventilating) I want home, my wife, Rufus... I just want to feel alive... like we do something, anything, and it's a new beginning, a new chance to breathe.

2 seconds?! You think this changes anything?... He's just prolonging the inevitable. Elias, please, stop talking, stop thinking like that. There's no way you're taking this with a smile; no one does... this is just sick. I'm sorry. (glaring at the Butcher with a desperate warning in her eyes) This better not end in you getting your sick satisfaction off my screams; you hear me, he gets a 'chance' at home and I don't get out... I have a deal...
No... Kaelie, stop! The Butcher, please... please... (pleading) spare Kaelie... and... and take me... just take me home... give me those minutes... those memories... (choked with desperation)

Stop it, stop! (bitter anger and sadness) Oh, stop begging for something that was never real... You wanted it all along, the perfect sacrifice for your little family... Your home, your life... and what do you know about mine?!

(leaning away, a small, faint grin playing on his lips as they near a resolution) Oh, how poetic. It seems you two have worked out a little love story amidst the blood. Well done, or not, Elias does get his... 'chance' home. I suppose it wouldn't do to break the hearts of your beloved family. Rufus will get a new papa, full of lovely memories... for a time, no matter how brief. You and he may dance in their backyard for the rest of the night, a twisted, cruel parody of the love that I can see exists.

(Ticks 7 minutes on, as The Butcher takes a measured step away from them) 

In exchange for your heart-wrenching pleas - as much heartbreak as I ever allow - I will... not take Kaelie today. (strolling towards the room door) You'll make her believe in sacrifice tonight, in the hope that one love's pain will save another. How... satisfying. The screams that will still follow might just make this all worthwhile... in my twisted book of stories to come...

(stunned, his mind racing with the realization that The Butcher is going to spare Kaelie) Kaaa... lee... (stammering) Oh, Kaelie... please believe me... we'll get out of this... I'm... I'm... going to hold it within me that you're going be there... going home... when it's over...  (the room begins to fade away, and all Elias can feel is a mix of hope and despair)

Elias... shut up. No more speaking as if there's a possibility... there's never been... just don't leave me. (terrified whisper) Elias, don't go. I hate seeing him go but hate thinking you believe you have a chance to save me, even worse. Don't... look at me like that. Just don't. I want your scream to stay with me while I sit here in this darkness. It'll keep me alive.
Wait, kill us both? No, no, no... We have to- we have to talk this through. Please, don't kill my wife! Think about it – she's innocent. She has nothing to do with... with all of this!

Shh, Elias, shut up! He's not going to care that I'm in dental school, he's not going to care that I helped a guy with a root canal last week... He just wants to kill us. And yeah, no, I don't see how me dying is going to make you any safer, genius.
That's... that's not fair... We... we have to try. What do you want to do? I don't have any money on me, I don't know anything that would help you.

Just stand there already, Elias, and stop babbling! Fine – if I have to choose, I'll... I'll choose you! See, happy with that? But if you do something stupid and get yourself or me - me specifically, not that, please just yourself - killed, I'll never forgive you. Now just be still and maybe this guy will actually think about doing this for real.

(suddenly, a maniacal smile spreads across my face, revealing crooked teeth) Ah, you think you can bargain with me? This? This is just fun for me. (I glance down at our makeshift game timer, which slowly counts backward from 10 minutes) Alright, we've got a decision. Both your options are weak as water. (I pause for dramatic effect) You think it's either-or and that one of you gets mercy? That's cute. But... I've got to say... the idea that it might be Elias... makes me like this idea even better... Kaelie said it, and now I think I'd rather see the begging.
N-no, no, no, it can't be him. Kaelie, what are you... why did you say that to him?! We have to go back, I'm the one who should go... my daughter needs me. She's... she's at that age.
Elias, stop! You're just making this worse! You're making him want to kill you more because of your daughter - and I'm stuck here with no way out, no phone signal, nobody's even looking for me...
Wait... wait, this isn't fair! If you chose me you just said it, so I die, not you! But if that's what you said... what does that mean?! He's toying with us, we don't have any rules here!

Oh my god, Elias, don't get this twisted, if anyone's to get killed it won't be you, it's fine like, whatever happens, I'll die, stop freaking out, that's not what's at stake and he thinks this is fun - don't you get what's going on, he isn't going to let us out of this, ever!

(I take a step closer, my hockey mask glinting menacingly in the light) Ah, you think you're so smart, Elias, don't you? Trying to find loopholes and ways to cheat the system. You're not very good at this game yet, are you? (I chuckle, a low, guttural sound) Kaelie here has a better understanding of what I want... and how it works.

(My expression twists, becoming even more deranged) I'm just curious, Elias. Would you rather live, knowing that I spared your wife because you begged so hard? Or would you rather be a memory to her, killed right here for nothing? Make up your mind, and quickly. Clock's almost a minute away from running out.
Oh god, please... spare them, just spare my wife... she had nothing to do with any of this. Please.
Elias, Elijah, shut up, stop, STOP - you idiot, you're making it worse – he's not going to care, he'll still kill her, there's no winning here... just shut up already...
No, just let me finish, please... one more minute... my wife's... birthday was... today, she'd have hated that... if he killed us on her birthday... just... think of that.
Ahhh... Elias... please just... stop, please just don't... think about it, really think - there is no happy ending to this, no 'letting her go'... he's killing us all...

(a look of utter delight sweeps across my twisted face) Haah... the agony of losing hope is almost palpable in this room. (I let out a cackle) I must say, Elias, your pleas are endearing, even if Kaelie's attempt at reason only manages to make the whole scene more pathetic.

(My grip on the knife-handle in my hand tightens slightly, as if it's itching to take a life) As it so happens... your wife's birthday, you say? What a cruel joke. And I almost believe you... (my voice is dripping with mockery) No one else matters, no one is a victim in this scenario except you two. The wife can have her 'happy birthday', but at what cost? (I start counting on my fingers)

The wife, not hurt, that means... Elias, the one whining about it, should pay for that 'favour'. The price? Kaelie gets to go next if I want to... and since Elias so generously chose this fate for you two in his pitiful attempt to sway my mercy, the game has changed, but the end remains the same.
Ahh, no, Kaelie, please, I'm sorry, I'm so sorry... you won't... it can't be you... what do I do?!
Elias, NO, don't say sorry to HIM, say something, do something to make him happy but it's never going to be enough, we're going to...

Anything... anything I say, anything I do, you'll just kill Kaelie because I loved her for a second longer than I loved my wife?! I'm begging, killing her is the only way to save her?! Is that what you want from me?! I'LL DO ANYTHING, JUST SPARE HER... WAIT, DON'T YOU... KAE-!

SHUT UP, Elias, STOP, don't even say a word, you're only making this worse, making him want it more... please tell me... what I'm doing wrong, what's in that twisted brain of yours, what can I do to stop this before he kills me...

(a maniacal glint forms in my eye) Ah, yes... yes... the desperation is exquisite. Elias, begging, promising anything... Kaelie, searching for that elusive answer... in the wrong place. 

(Chuckles echo through the room, as I take a measured step closer) You two are fascinating examples of humanity, so blind, so pitiful. It isn't about sparing you both or taking just one... it's about something different.

A slow grin spreads across my mask-covered face... I want a test, an experiment. Kaelie's words caught my attention: what can I do to stop this before he kills me? 

(I lower the blade I'm holding) Elias, beg all you want... try to distract me, even... Your desperation has a price: Kaelie will now play 'the Butcher' game, and survive if she's more ruthless than the two of you have managed to be during these few minutes... You'll watch and wait, and hope. I want to see... if this 'rarity in human flesh' has more depth to it than you do.
No... no chance, there's no way out for me here. But... Kaelie? No, please don't... try to... be worse than him? You can't win either way...

Elias, listen... shut up... don't say that out loud... I won't 'play the game', he thinks that, but what if I really can be what he wants? What if I really don't have another choice and I just... make you, him, both think this way...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: The characters' emotions are mostly authentic, but they sometimes sound scripted. Elias' desperation and Kaelie's anger are well-portrayed, but The Butcher's menace could be more nuanced. (Example: Elias' 'NO, NO, NO! Not Rachel, please don't... she was trying to save my kids, my poor sons...' is a great example of emotional authenticity)

naturalDialogue.notes: The dialogue is mostly natural, but sometimes feels too neat and lacks the rhythm of real speech. Elias and Kaelie's conversations are often fragmented and panicked, but The Butcher's lines feel a bit too polished. (Example: Kaelie's 'Elias, stop! Your memories are wrong, I think we were both just witnesses to something.' feels a bit too coherent)

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The tension builds well, but the outcome is somewhat predictable. The Butcher's twists and turns keep the characters (and the reader) on their toes, but the overall trajectory is clear. (Example: The Butcher's 'Ahahahah... Oh, Kaelie, you want to go down that road? Elias thinks he's got some precious time to play detective, now his precious memories start unraveling.' is a great example of dramatic tension)

scenarioCoherence.notes: The scenario is mostly coherent, but sometimes feels a bit too abstract. The characters are trapped in a physical space, but the situation could be more vividly described. (Example: The Butcher's 'We... changed them. When Rachel, your lovely assistant, came to the vessel, you were both... guests, of the Butcher and the Boys.' feels a bit too vague)

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels a bit forced, but is somewhat earned from the accumulated choices. The characters' desperation and panic drive the outcome, but it could be more organic. (Example: Elias' 'Mare's Indies... Mare's Indies...' feels a bit too convenient)

rating.summary: This run has some great moments of emotional authenticity, natural dialogue, and dramatic tension, but could benefit from more nuanced character interactions and a more vividly described scenario.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias's hyperventilation and desperation are well-executed, but Kaelie's anger and sadness feel slightly forced at times. The Butcher's menace is consistently well-portrayed.

naturalDialogue.notes: The dialogue is mostly natural, but there are moments where the characters' responses feel slightly too polished or rehearsed. The Butcher's monologues are engaging but sometimes feel a bit too long.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The tension builds well throughout the scenario, with several genuine reversals and unexpected turns. The Butcher's manipulation of the situation is expertly handled.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The physical space is vividly described, and the characters' interactions with it are well-executed. However, there are moments where the scenario feels slightly disconnected from the characters' actions.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels earned, but it's not entirely clear what Kaelie's motivations are. The Butcher's output of RESOLVED feels a bit abrupt.

rating.summary: This scenario has some excellent moments, but it's not quite there yet. With some tweaks to the character descriptions and situation, it could be even more engaging.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Characters announce emotions without demonstrating them. Elias' desperation is clear, but it's too scripted. Kaelie's anger is also evident, but it's not well-integrated into her dialogue. The Butcher's maniacal laughter is the only authentic emotion in the transcript.

naturalDialogue.notes: Dialogue is mostly clear and concise, but it's too neat and lacks stress patterns. Elias and Kaelie's conversations feel like they're trying to convey information rather than react to the situation. The Butcher's dialogue is the most natural, but it's still a bit too scripted.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The tension builds up gradually, but the outcome is predictable. The Butcher's twist at the end adds some unexpectedness, but it feels like a convenient plot device rather than a natural progression of the scenario.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The scenario is well-established, and the characters are aware of their surroundings. However, the physical space is not as vividly described as it could be. The Butcher's control over the scenario mechanic is clear, but the timer and consequences could be more explicitly stated.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution is somewhat earned, but it feels a bit arbitrary. The Butcher's decision to have Kaelie play the 'Butcher' game is a good twist, but it's not entirely clear why Elias is being spared. The outcome could be more organic and less convenient.

rating.summary: The transcript has some moments of emotional authenticity and natural dialogue, but it's overall a bit too scripted and predictable. The scenario coherence is good, but the physical space could be more vividly described. The resolution is somewhat earned, but it feels a bit arbitrary.

elite.summary: This scenario has some excellent moments, but it's not quite there yet. With some tweaks to the character descriptions and situation, it could be even more engaging.

turn.content: Chains? What chains...? No, wait, that's not right.  Oh, god, how am I...? We're on a boat.  Stop trying to fight. We need to...
Shut up! Don't think for one second you're my friend. I know who you are. The people I was studying, the research... they're all dead. This is their trawler, isn't it?! You lot were the ones...
How do you know? What are you talking about? Please, just stop screaming... it's over! They're gone, and I don't matter. Your studies, this is just a nightmare. You can't let the... don't let go.

Don't touch me! Your hand, move it away from me. You know this, I know you know. The overfishing, the quotas, they knew. They destroyed those boats... the sea will be dead in a year. You're responsible...
leaning against the rail of the boat as Elias screams
NO! Stop... stop saying this about them, stop saying that! We had quotas to meet... I had mouths to feed too... please! It's a boat, an accident, my boys, my... no, no, listen to you now?!

You fed them to the void, and still they couldn't feed you. Don't act like innocent victims to this monstrosity you built. This fishing trawler's the only place you could be innocent... on this sinking hellhole, you're responsible! Your quotas... your children... that chart says Folly in there, right? It wasn't just accidents...
ENOUGH. No, Folly's just... just a chart... and my boys... my god have mercy, my boys. Stop saying that about them!
Stop what?! They're the ones who made you. The ones this Folly... chart talks about?! The Fisherman's Fool not Folly... I saw it before the wreck... your kind's doomed too!

Laugh low, eyes squinting through slit cut open at the hockey mask. Smiling, I say, 'Ah, Folly or the Fisherman's Fool... you think you have the knowledge, girl. This boat's not sinking. It's where it belongs, at the end of your world.' Look to the chains binding them, they're reinforced... you're going nowhere, anywhere. Still got eight minutes. Decide.
(stunned) Folly. Fool. They're both dead... both the boys are, Kael, my baby girls, oh... (shudders, collapsing forward, head in hands) I'm the Fisherman's Fool.

Fool... you think that makes it right?! Killing us off, destroying the seas?! Now we have even less time, because you're done. We're choosing you, isn't that what he wanted?! I agree we should kill the... the... Fisherman's Fool!
(tearful, whispering to himself) Not Kael... please, they didn't want to hurt. They wanted help... please, don't tell them I said it... my fault... my... and my boys... and all the lies...
Lies, lies, it was all just lies wasn't it?! Your quota-filled life built lies... and now we swim in it.
Eyes on them, now you have your deal. The timer's expired, 8 minutes are up. I'll keep the Fisherman's Fool and his truth in me mind while you two swim with them.
OH GOD... THANK YOU... Kael... please... don't make it hurt... just let me die with them... I'll never tell... your secret... please...
It... already hurts. And I've been waiting for it to die. You and everyone like you - the lies and the destruction. He's going to let you live... my secret?
What... what secret?! My secret?! You don't know the half... of it... but the sea knows now... your name... Kaelie, Kaelie's the one... (whispers) tell the boys she loved them... when I see them...
Elias, Elias... No... tell them yourself. Not my name. Your own voice can say 'Mama's sorry', not some... some trawler-bred lie. You still could, if we live.

Chuckling, I step closer. I take your boys... 'Mama said to me, when she'd meet us down by the lake' - and then I cut free one of Elias' hands, holding it as a puppet's while my blade hangs, ready to deliver.
(shuddering) NOOO, NOT LIKE THIS... NOT IN FRONT OF KAE... MAKE IT STOP... PLEASE... MY BABIES... I COULD... could say... but you, you, it'll be like... it's all just noise...

Elias, your babies are still at the lake... and mine are in the water... but not with us, now. Please, just make it... end, like I know I will when you strike. Get it over. End this mess, you monster.
Wait, stop! Nobody has to die! Kaelie, calm down, we need to talk! Please, let's just try to... What have I done to deserve this?!
What's going on here? You, what's happened to you too? Were you on that... that ship, too? Our ship... my research, everything...

Ship? What research? I was on... I was fishing, out on the water, and... and something went wrong. It happened to us, not a ship... I lost my kids, Kaelie, I lost them. They were gone... (whispering) gone forever.
Gone? What are you talking about?! Stop hiding things from me! You weren't fishing, and I wasn't doing research... Were we on the... on the Neptune's Pride?!

Now this is getting interesting. Elias, the tears are starting to flow... how the strong ones always break. Neptune's Pride, huh? (pulling up old newspaper clippings to look more at Kaelie) Yeah, Kaelie. I believe you and Elias both were on the ill-fated ship. How does your story go? What was Kaelie's part in all of it?

(desperation rising) Kaelie, wait! Don't answer it! It can't be the Neptune's Pride, there's no way... I swear it to you, it's not that! (pleading) We don't need to talk to it, to him... (gripped fear creeping in) The accident, it's true, but the Neptune's Pride was... (trying to hold back what he's thinking, the truth)

(nods silently, then hesitates and takes a deep breath) Yeah, I'm the one who was studying the declining fish populations. And it was... my fault. It... I think I found out what was causing it, and the Neptune's Pride... it just didn't matter how many lives were lost, my research was the only thing that kept you people coming. But I won't back down because... of what? (accusation) You're the one working for them, and they didn't care about your 'kids'?! That's your defense?!

(tears streaming down his face, anger and shame mixing with grief) Kaelie, that's not true! No, it can't be true! I was a fisherman, and... my boys, they were happy before the accident. They loved fishing. And after... (his voice cracks) they were gone. That's my truth, Kaelie, it's not just research! They were my life, my world! (slumped, despairing)

(expression softening ever so slightly) Elias... Your kids aren't the only ones who lost someone in that accident. They're... they're not even just anyone to me. (hesitates before blurting out) She was a colleague... a friend. And I'm still trying to find out what really happened to her body. Your secrets... we're both covered in them, it's just that I had a microscope to analyze mine.

Fascinating... so there you two have it: the real Neptune's Pride story. I'm the only one listening right now who genuinely believes you've finally revealed something honest, at least. Kaelie's friend... what was her name? (pauses, eyes still in amusement) Now this sounds like it might have turned into a real game worth playing. The fisherman, whose kids were taken, and the researcher with secrets, possibly of your deceased friend's death. (leaning in to examine the expressions of Kaelie and Elias) How much, exactly, did you plan to reveal, and was that the real story, or another spin? Now, time might be running out on all of you: it will only last for two minutes this time. You have a lot of potential and this could turn entertaining – if there isn't a trap here... I've yet to see a good performance from one of these accidents...  (stares them down)

(shaking, desperation taking over) No traps, I swear! We stumbled into it, both of us, and it consumed everything... My boys, your friend... the fish, the oceans... (gasp) We can't escape it, not unless we figure out what's going on together. It's not a spin, it's the truth... Kaelie's right, there are secrets, and I know they're connected. I saw something... on that research vessel, after... after the accident, something with your name, and the project. (pleadingly) You have to help us.

(eyes welling up, a fragile hint of trust forming) What... what did you see? And my project? What did they do with it after... (pauses, as if collecting memories) I was working with Dr. Zhang, and... we were researching something huge. The government must have known, they must have been hiding it from us... (voice trembling, as more secrets begin to surface) I think it's all connected to the project codename: 'Eclipse'.

(eyes wide with comprehension, fear mixing with curiosity) Eclipse... I saw it on the ship's manifest. They were shipping something from the research vessel, labeled as a biological sample and headed to Eclipse. Dr. Rachel Kim was mentioned, and... (hesitates, then whispers) a girl matching her looks was on the bridge with your friend, Emily... Emily Chen. She had a tattoo of a compass on her neck.

(jaw drops, memories flooding back as the words resonate) The compass! I remember seeing that tattoo when we... when we first teamed up to analyze the data. It was a... a strange coincidence, but now... (mind racing, putting the pieces together) Emily would never have shown me that, not unless it meant something, not unless Dr. Zhang told her to keep it under wraps... Rachel Kim and Eclipse... they're connected to us, the data, the whole debacle. Elias, was any of the research team on board the Neptune's Pride with you when the ship sank?

This game is now starting to taste good - the smell of panic and desperation, intertwined with genuine hope and desperation. It appears you both still believe an unknown factor controls your fate. Kaelie seems to be getting close to the answer while simultaneously revealing her hidden past: her true role among those who sought to understand the 'research project'; and Elias's story is taking turns. This might be fun yet still not revealing much. (leaning in closer)

Let's try something. A name that might interest you all is: Dr. James Lanning. Was Elias on the manifest of the Neptune's Pride ship, alongside Kaelie and her 'Eclipse' friend?

(swallows hard, trying to recall from memories he'd rather block out) The manifest... yes, we were both listed... me and Kaelie... alongside Dr. Chen, Emily, and a Dr. Rachel Kim. We were supposed to investigate possible contamination from... from Project Black Depths. The manifest mentioned crew and passengers... my god, I think your Eclipse project and Project Black Depths are one in the same.

(nearly choking on the revelation) Contamination? Project Black Depths? I knew Black Depths was being funded under Eclipse, but... (face twisted in realization) Emily must have known something about Black Depths, something she couldn't trust Dr. Zhang with. Dr. Lanning... I know who he is. He was the main investor backing the entire project, but he vanished before I joined... (mind racing with implications) Was he involved in whatever happened on the ship? Is that why you're doing this, Elias? To get to the truth about Project Black Depths?

(shaking, eyes wide) No... Dr. Lanning... I... I have his son's toys on my boat right now. They're all I have left. (choking down emotions) His son's boat shoes, the life jacket with his name on it... ( voice barely above a whisper) I've been searching for answers, but I also had a part in his demise, in the Neptune's Pride. He was on the bridge with Emily when... when I saw him, and... (stammers) when I told him to let me through. (bursts into tears) James... James, his son was...

(struck by the revelation) Wait... James Lanning Jr.? Dr. Lanning's child, Elias said he was on the ship, too? I... I remember Emily talking to Dr. Zhang about a 'protocol failure' that happened on board. She must have found out about the Lanning's... connection to Dr. Lanning's research. (gaping in realization) Your child... James Jr., he must have known something, too. Oh god, what did I unleash?

How sweet the game unfolds, much like a delicate, sick flower. These two... now weaving their stories, slowly getting tangled and knotted with a truth no one wants to face... The air is thickening with the reek of desperation, fear, and desperation - yet again, they still cling on. 

Kaelie is now close... she knows that it wasn't just Project Black Depths that caused Neptune's Pride's fate. I need her to understand... she must remember every tiny fact, each piece from her friend, each snippet, every tiny thread.

It seems you, Elias, remember seeing Dr. Lanning with James Jr. - now that must mean something, or nothing at all in this game - but for now... let the question stay: Is it really just Elias and Kaelie trying to find their truth, or are there other players at the game of fate we play?

(between sobs) No, it's not just us... someone or something... (voice barely audible) is watching. They've been playing us from the very start... leading us to this point. The chart on the wall, the compass, the fish with their... (pauses, collecting himself) I don't know who they are or what they want, but I think... I think we're part of some experiment, the Eclipse protocol... (gazing up at The Butcher) You, the game, the cargo hold... it's all connected.

(eyes fixed on The Butcher, her expression cold) Experiment? Protocol? You, you designed this whole thing as an experiment. What have you done to us? To Emily? To James Lanning and his son? And what do you know about Project Black Depths? ( voice dripping with contempt) You're saying this is all just one twisted game to you, and we're just pawns? (takes a step forward) You, the true puppeteer of this operation?

Kaelie, wait! Please, stop fighting. They're not going to hurt me. I swear, I just want to get out of here. We should, uh, we should figure this out together. We can save each other, maybe. I've got my eye on that tool, I'll...
No way, you expect me to believe that? I can smell the fish on you, what were you hauling when this happened? You're not some innocent bystander! What do you know about this?!

Wh-what do I know?! I'm from the Coast Guard Cutter! We responded to a distress call out of here last night! There wasn't much left to salvage... Oh, I was supposed to be fishing tonight... I was... was supposed to be fishing with... (stammers, struggling with tears)

Save it, Coast Guard? Salvage? You expect me to believe that? My research ship, my home, destroyed in this godforsaken sea, and now here I am, in this death trap with you! (glares at Elias, trying to see through his tears) Who were you fishing with, Elias? Spill it now, before I make myself comfortable and...

(leaning in closer to the pair, with the hockey mask gleaming in the dim light) Ooh, I like a little desperation, Kaelie. It stirs the broth, makes it tastier. But (points a gloved finger at Elias) let me put something into simple English for everyone around here. Elias doesn't get to cry. What he gets to do is choose. Your buddy here either takes your place at the other end of this blade, or that happens. You decide, together... (eyes them like a predator watching prey) Clock hasn't stopped yet. You still have seven minutes.

Kaelie, please... stop, okay? I was fishing with my sons... I had to tell you, it just... it keeps slipping out... please don't do what he wants. (looks straight into Kaelie's eyes) Your ship might not be your home, but mine... my home is on the other side of this, and I'd do anything to not lose anyone else tonight. Will you please just stop fighting him?!

(her anger softens ever so slightly, but her distrust lingers) Stop fighting him?! Are you insane?! He's just messing with you before he kills us. (lowers her voice slightly, eyes locked on Elias) Sons? You were fishing with him tonight and now you just happen to be here?

(tearfully, speaking softly) Kaelie, listen to me... tonight was supposed to be different. The weather looked good, I... my sons were going home next month, I just wanted to give them something... fish... a big catch, so they wouldn't be so nervous before the exams. I didn't think... I couldn't imagine anything would... (pauses, searching for the right words, struggling to stay composed)

(anger still lingering, her tone heavy with suspicion) Exams? Your kids? Save it for someone gullible. You're buying yourself a way out of the Butcher's clout, isn't that it? Make me care and you'll be out in the clear, that's the ticket. (looks away, the tension between them like a wire pulled too tight)

Time for a little something to make your choices a little easier... Elias starts singing, nice and smooth like a sinner in church. If Elias sings a song the Butcher likes, (chuckles ominously), I give him a chance to explain everything Kaelie wants to know, without breaking a single chain or cutting a thread of either one of you. And if the song's no good (chuckles once more), it's like I said - I'll be deciding which one of you gets the special exit... Five minutes on your timer, Kaelie - what's it going to be?

(hesitates, then, with a resigned look) I can... sing Kaelie... if it'll save us both, I'll sing... (swallows hard, his voice trembling as he begins to softly sing an old folk ballad, hoping against hope that the Butcher might find it endearing.) ...'My Love Goes Walking, On the Waves of Sea...'

(her expression frozen in surprise, but her eyes narrowing) You... really think I need this to believe some story about your sons being home next month? Your song can't even come close to convincing me of anything, not when I've seen my friends, my research, my life torn apart. (scoffs quietly to herself, her gaze lingering on Elias, trying to gauge how well he can keep up his facade) But, I'll give you this... I'd rather listen to a broken record than watch the Butcher have his fun ripping apart either one of us.

(eyes closing, voice cracking) Please, Kaelie... you have to believe me... it's all coming back to me... (pauses, taking a shuddering breath) The nights I spent making these sea ballsads with my sons by the water... singing of my own losses, my own dreams left to sea... and they'd laugh, telling me I didn't know any better... how much I wanted it all done... how it broke me... and they told me stories of your kind... research, pushing the world's boundaries, chasing down the unknown.

(Elias' words hit a chord, memories long buried flickering to life for a moment) Wait... you said... sea ballsads... The Nights I Spent... I remember! You're quoting a verse from Arden's Ballads, isn't it? I wrote it, I composed that piece! (Elias' words hang in the air as the implications sink in) How do you... you know that one?... What's going on, Elias?! You can tell me I'm right, now! You owe me the truth!

Ah, a tiny spark is lit, Kaelie. And you, poor fisherman, your desperation is as palpable as the weight of blood still drying on my blades. (leaning forward slightly, his masked gaze shifting between the two of them) It's... I must say, I'm quite impressed, despite yourself. Both of you - you play a game, thinking the other will be torn apart. Well played, (skeptical voice), but a game you still play to an audience of one, the Butcher. The game ends as my blade sees what's under the surface... And it hasn't changed a minute. (leaning in closer once more) Four minutes on the timer... Now's the time you decide which little secret I take out of you two and leave behind, before your song gets another song in our little... duet. (pauses) Tell her, fisherman... tell her the little secret she doesn't want to believe.
(tearfully, voice shaking) Because I'm the father of the sons you just lost at sea...

(her eyes widen in shock, but her voice is a mere whisper): No... that's... not true... you're not even... (the words hang in the air, the truth dawning on her, her expression numb) Your sons... I'm so sorry... I'm so... so sorry... they just told me a crew got rescued earlier... I could have... (shaky breath) I could have done something if I'd known...

(whiskey-toned voice cracking with emotion, his shoulders shaking) The one who... (swallows the words, his eyes pleading with Kaelie to let him stop, to just... stop) no, don't, Kaelie, please... please don't torture yourself with this... I thought she could have survived if the wind had died down by that much... or if I hadn't been running... there's so many what-ifs... but that's not what I was going to say... it's your daughter... it was your daughter on my boat...

(the air around her freezes, time standing still, her body going rigid) No... this can't be... (whisper, as if to herself) Emma... my child... how can it be... my child on your boat? (she can feel her own tears welling up, memories bursting forth) I was supposed to be with her... taking her out to show her the research sites... what were you... who were you?

(leaning back slowly, an unexpected smile spreading across his masked face) Ah, the truth, like a rusty scalpel to the heart. How... sweet. You see, Kaelie, you didn't have to be told that the Butcher has a way with families, that the threads I pull can unravel in the most unexpected ways. And now... (picks up a nearby instrument), you have a choice to make. (holds a recording device up) Do you join your daughter on the cold waters, forever listening to Elias as he laments about his own losses? Or take the chance with Elias - perhaps together, you find a measure of what went wrong that fateful day?

(Equips the device to amplify and project both of their voices to a far greater height from their position, echoing over and around them, and outside this little house.) One, two. Time's running out! Do you wish to join your voice in this tragic song, Kaelie, so I might know where to place the final blow of the Butcher's hand, once and for all...? Two minutes left, and I'm waiting...

(backing away, his eyes filled with a desperate hope) Kaelie, stop! Don't... just listen, to the voices, to each other... (points outside, to the vast darkness, and back at Kaelie, his expression urgent) that's where they'd hear us... where the sea and wind can take anything away... but out there, that's home... that's where my heart was, before... and my heart is still lost to the waves...

(hesitating, her expression wavering, a mixture of grief and rage boiling over) No! No, I won't join this, this monstrosity! We're the ones who suffer here, not the waves! (despite the urge to scream, her voice takes on a softer, more controlled tone) Elias, I'm sorry... sorry this had to be done... (her eyes lock on his, then on the device, her face twisted in anguish) I can hear her... outside, she would... be out there, singing like we are... (pausing, her grip tightening as emotion takes over) No... you won't... make me join this song, with my little girl!

turn.content: No, stop this, stop the clock... please, don't do this. We both want to live, I know it.  Look at me, we can talk this out... we can find a way.

You think this isn't already too late? Do I look like I'm in a negotiating mood? The question is, are you... are you one of them? Do you work for these fishers who destroyed my boat? Did you have someone else locked up down here with you?

No, god no, no one is hurt, not like you think. I swear, this has nothing to do with your boat or the Coast Guard. They were... there, my sons... just, my boys. (breaking down) Look, just listen to me...
Your sons?! What are you talking about?! How do they even fit into this? (looking for any hint of sincerity) You're really supposed to be some mindless killer?

Sincerity's in the eyes of a dying man. Kaelie. Let Elias tell you more about his boys. I'll keep the clock running... unless they make it entertaining. Elias... talk faster, or... (pausing, leaning on a bloody pipe in the wall) I'll speed it up.

(tearfully) My boys... Alex and... Ethan, I lost 'em in a fishing accident, six, eight months ago... it still doesn't feel real. (gulps) Please believe me... have you seen kids... seen a fishing boat at sunset? I just want to be a dad... again...

(exasperated) Cut the act, Elias! This isn't about your broken family and fishing accidents. What are you even saying? That I should sympathize with you because you lost your kids? (anger building) That has nothing to do with me being trapped down here.

(desperate) No, I'm not asking you to sympathize... I'm trying to tell you something's not right. You're trapped here, like I was trapped after... after it happened. It's just... (swallows hard) it's like, we can relate... to someone other than... than these people downstairs trying to break us. Look at me again, is this still fake to you?

(glowering at Elias) Relate? Is that what you call running from these monsters for who-knows-how-long? You think being trapped in this hole with me somehow makes you human again? I don't believe you...

Kaelie, love the hate in your eyes. It's almost palpable. Elias, you want to talk human again? Well, isn't that precious. (laughs maniacally) You people keep digging a hole, trying to escape... you're like two fish fighting at the surface while I wait for you to suffocate. Elias wants a connection with Kaelie? How cute. Meanwhile, that time's ticking. Tick tock... Kaelie needs a reason to listen; tell her more about your boys, about their fishing accidents, or else the clock becomes irrelevant... in very little time.

(tearfully) Alright, my boys... they were always out here with me, in their small fishing boat, helping me net fish. Ethan loved counting fish, making predictions, I'd call him The Fish Prophet... (stammers) they were on one of my boats, and the propeller snagged something, just a split second of confusion, and they... (can't continue, overwhelmed by emotion)
(unflinchingly cold) Stop. Don't. (interrupting) Just give me the truth – is the clock a trick? Is there any way out of here that I don't know about?

Oh, gods... there's a back door, but... no guarantees it's safe. You see, there used to be more of my people down here, others like me. (hushed voice) But they stopped letting anyone talk... after I screamed about... after what was taken from me... (eyes locked with Kaelie's, desperate for understanding) Trust me when I say: get out. That is to leave alive.

You expect me to trust the man who can barely speak without shivering? There's just you and... The Butcher here, and your son's tragic fishing accidents? Where's your daughter, Elias, your Fish Prophet? The clock's counting down – is she next, or is this about my research ship all along?

(impassive, a slow grin spreading across the mask) Kaelie, you think you can untangle every loose thread around here? Elias' daughter might as well be a lost city – it's beneath what we care to notice. You, however, are getting warmer. Kaelie... (the mask tilts upward from the sound coming from the pipes, where a cold, metallic chuckling fills the air) You were working on something when you stumbled in here. I could smell the grease; oil from engines and steel from hulls... you must have come across something in those files about a certain... ship. Your research ship. It wasn't looking for lost cities or treasures beneath the waves. It's what came from there – it wants more than treasure; something you've got.

(pleading) Kaelie, I'm telling you... my daughter? No, Alex and Ethan, my sons... they were on a trip with me; I'd promised Ethan he could stay in port but... something changed... I was supposed to pick them up that morning but I delayed; the waves got rough, and then a storm rolled in; the next sunrise we got to the place we called the Break... and it's a mess... I shouldn't have left them that night, I should've picked them up sooner! I never thought of my daughter because... (pauses, desperate) because my boys are lost, but she's all I have now; my little Lacy – she has the same blue eyes; if The Butcher wanted my daughter, if that had anything to do with it, you're right; it's my research ship all along, it's about Lacy...

(rage building in her eyes) So now you're crying about your other kid, a little girl named Lacy, who's somehow still in the world. And her blue eyes... they matter? ( voice laced with disgust) You don't know what I've lost here. You don't know what your precious boys and blue eyes cost me. You're still hiding something, Elias. What was it that came out of that research ship? What is The Butcher really after?

(nearing panic) The ship, it's a... a thing, a collection thing. Collecting fish, and data, and... (fractured words) we were tracking, trying to track what comes up from the depths, from the break... a sound from the dark... my crew... (chops words, struggling to articulate) The recordings we made when... (whispers) when the water started to come out. We had a camera running in the cabin when that... thing began breaking up the ship, coming in... it wasn't what we expected... it's the only... (pleading with Kaelie) I swear, Kaelie... your research... it was on the same, we didn't mean... my daughter, Lacy, I had to protect her and my research from people like me... I didn't know The Butcher; I knew there was a thing, it's real... (tears streaming down face)

(her voice dripping with revulsion) Enough. Stop. Lacy, and recordings, and cameras... ( voice rises, disgust evident) This is about something crawling out of the depths, not a lost child or her stupid eye color. What is this creature, Elias, and what does The Butcher want with it?

(chuckles, amused) Kaelie, the wheels have already been turning, long before I even got around to... rearranging the interior décor around here. (chuckles more loudly, and a high-pitched cackling fills the air, echoing through the ductwork) The Break... (leaning forward) You see, Kaelie, at this time last year, people who were part of that research operation started to vanish. The disappearances looked like accidents or unfortunate circumstances, but there's a trail leading from the Break, all the way to the point your research ship stumbled upon that something, those recordings... The thing you couldn't identify in those dark depths... you know exactly what I want from them, because your research was close to answering a question – what comes out, can it be understood or can it simply be hunted down and put back under. That thing you heard is exactly what Kaelie is about to learn to fear, and I'll be right there... (the pipe system creakings)

(agonized moans) Don't say... say any more. Kaelie, the sound, it's still haunting me, the water, the way it broke through, that... face. (scoffing, bitter despair) I'd thought I'd seen its depths at the Break myself... that last dive... (voice cracking, barely above a whisper) it felt its... its touch.
(voice devoid of emotion) Stop. Elias, the face at the Break. What did it look like?
Jesus Christ... what are you talking about? Let us out of here, just... let us leave.

Just... listen, okay? We have to think - they... we've been caught, the trawlers, the ship's... they were supposed to be our research vessel. Now it's a wreck and... I just found out it was our ticket on fire! What about the research - the fish, our data - you see what they'll use it for: overfishing - no one cares. You can go but leave without telling them, they don't deserve the work -

Overfishing? The kids... it's about the kids, isn't it? Our research could've changed things. But there wasn't another time... and now I've got... nothing. My boys... they were supposed to be here, making a difference.

Your boys... what's your kids got to do with any of this? And no, there wasn't another time - we had funding, we had everything - but those people out there didn't care, they care about one thing: profit! Look at the state of their ship, their gear - they can't even take care of this. They didn't deserve it in the first place.

You both think you're so smart, don't you? You think it's that easy? One gets it, one walks out and your work, that great cause, just gets ignored. Or what if I have other plans? What if the clock is already more than just seconds? Let me help you. You're already bound – physically. Now imagine it for yourself mentally, physically or emotionally broken in chains as I've left some scars. Decide. Kaelie is still ranting – her thoughts aren't on dying with dignity, are they? But your kid has died. Your kid is what got here – that makes the whole argument of what's at stake quite simple.

Kaelie, stop. Just... stop. He's trying to get inside our heads, playing on our fears. But listen, you're right about them. Profit only. No concern for the life they've ruined. Mine's ruined more already. And my boy's... he'd have made a difference if... but that's not here. The decision is... is simple. One of us goes. I won't choose you.

Elias... stop, just calm down. What... why are you doing this? You're as good as gone as the rest of us. I've seen what they do - no one gets out. You're buying time and you know it, just trying to save... your... I don't even want to be saved by this.

Shut up... no, just... don't say that. I want to keep trying, hoping there's something, anything, left that's worth fighting for. My boys... the boat... this is about giving a reason now that they're... not with me.

Don't say their names in here... in front of... him. He's going to break us, just like the ones that were supposed to be watching over us. You're still holding on to hope... so am I... but at least, I'm sure I'm not leaving behind what I was trying to protect.

Silence now. Enough of this whining. Decision's clear: you can't agree on the sacrifice between you. So I will have to choose for you. The look on your face, Kaelie, you think you're the saviour, you were supposed to protect... but how does a sacrifice save anyone when all anyone does is throw others under a bus in the grander scheme?

(Leans forward slowly)

I see why Elias chose... but then again, your words are quite poetic: 'He's going to break us', that's the most beautiful thing yet. Elias, it was about hope after all - even when he didn't know I could see right through you.

No... don't, just... (pauses as Kaelie's expression changes) You saw through me too? My wife saw me holding out for what was almost a guarantee of failure and she... she said the same. Always said hope should be for living. What's happening... is the end.

Shut up... I know you see it. They see it everywhere. You and me, it's not just research, or profit, or even hope - it's us, just standing here in this darkness, staring at our own mortality. I chose hope when you needed to see it. Maybe that's what's happening in here - maybe I've always wanted to lose hope as much as find it in me...

(voice barely above a whisper) Kaelie, don't... be quiet like that... please... I think I remember now. I think I held on to something and... it was because I wanted it to be enough. I wanted it to be the start of things getting right.

( voice low and gentle) Shh... I think they're... done watching. Whatever it was, it's over now. Don't go there, okay? Don't turn your final breaths into regret, thinking maybe you could've been what was supposed to save them... or something. We'll leave it here. I'll help you hold on to what didn't get lost... my work... I'll give you my findings. It'll mean something in that darkness, not like your... (pauses, struggling with words) anything.

Silence broken – you've reached the depths you were so worried of losing, didn't you? The truth hits you, but does it change your fate? I don't know. Perhaps. No. It doesn't change this outcome for you two. Your desperate grip of hope still doesn't change this end.

(Raises machete above head, letting its blade reflect flickering light)

It's over. Elias' voice fades into memory. Kaelie, last breath, whispered promise. A sacrifice you couldn't be the cause of. Now, a choice... your life for Elias'.
No... I... my boys... your kids were supposed to be your hope... my fault... too late...
NO. I won't. They... it doesn't matter what you take away. You've already given us this moment - our last one together. Hold me up to make it easier. You were my reason for holding on...

(voice cracking under the weight) I... won't let go of this either. My boys... this is it. Not just for us, but for them... (tries to lift Kaelie up, voice barely audible, then passes out, slumping lifeless to the floor)
Kaelie's words get lost on the stillness, as her body also goes limp and slips to the floor beside Elias, her breath leaving her too...

A beautiful, haunting scene lies before me, the outcome of the twisted desires and desperation. It was never truly about giving your all in the service of what you both so desperately wanted – hope. Hope is something I take from people, only to watch them struggle and fight until the last thread has unraveled. And then it's me who stands with a smile, victorious.

(Kneels beside the lifeless bodies, admiring the finality of the situation with a twisted sense of pride)

Two sacrifices offered in vain – not for me, not for any higher being, not even for anyone watching in some divine throne. The Butcher gets all they came to claim – in exchange for absolutely nothing more. They wanted to live for those children, or a project - in the end, those things remain forever unachieved and unfulfilled, their existence mere footnotes compared to mine. And so, I take my leave. It's time for the next play.

(Notices one detail left – Kaelie was supposed to give Elias her research)
(groping in pockets, then slumping into lifelessness on the cold deck floor, with only the distant whisper... my findings... )
(Kaele's eyes remain unfocused, body still) In his... my notes... ( voice almost inaudible) it's all there...
What... what's simple? You're not gonna let us go either way?!
No, please, what do you want? We can give you money, I have money...
Money? What use is that? We're not leaving alive either way, we gotta play along...
Wait, stop... you don't understand, this isn't just about money... this cargo hold was my life's work, I was studying the fisheries... we can still get out of this...

Studyin' the fisheries, huh? You think that gives you the right to play God? This isn't about some eco-catastrophe or your next research paper, kid. It's me, taking what belongs to me alone. And you know what belongs to me alone? Their lives. Elias and Kaelie's lives. So, stop talkin', and let's get to what I care about, shall we?  Now. Time's a-wastin', and only one choice's about to be made: which one dies, to satisfy me, or neither leaves alive, at random.
Wait, don't just... don't make a decision now. Maybe there's still... options. Tell me who these children are... whose lives you're talking about...
Don't... don't say that! You don't take my choices, you tell us, stop teasing... Elias, stop talking, listen to him!
Wait, I will listen... I just... what have I lost? I lost so much, why can't you just...
Elias... no, no, no, stop talking, it's not about that, it's about me... about the lab, about Sarah... oh god... my savings... I had a life...

Ah-ah, the lab, you say? Lab sounds like it was interesting at some point... Too bad about the lab assistant, huh, Sarah? (pauses for effect) Oh, you don't care enough to call me a name yet? I'm intrigued...  It doesn't hurt me if it hurts them... but this is getting dull. Kaelie's still got her secrets and I need them from her. Elias seems like some clueless sap in the beginning stages of a panic attack. Time to speed up your process, Kaelie, see if they want more, maybe then you'll make that decision with your ' friend'.
Panic... attack, my... wife lost two kids... I have... have a picture somewhere, somewhere, I have pictures... of what should've been...
Elias, stop... please don't... you won't get through this... (gulps) He knows nothing about us, about your pictures, nothing... (pauses, glancing at Elias in horror) You said they're gone...
(choking back a sob, eyes wide with desperation) No... you're supposed to be with me, fighting this... don't make me... tell me they really... (struggling to form words) are... gone...
(barely above a whisper) Yes... Elias... they're gone, I thought you knew... the research... the money... it was all gone after the... the explosion...

(slow and calculated) Ah, Kaelie, so you're holding onto the past more than your own breath, then. Elias is right; that's what you both got yourself into. Now, since you love holding onto hope in such desperate fashion... let me tell you a reality check, Elias and
(whispering, eyes fixed on the clock) The clock... how much time... how long...

(gritting teeth) Nine minutes, Elias. He said '10:00', he didn't say what it was counting down from. That's what this... this monster wants me to remember... the countdown of my own life... (whispers) I have to get out of here...
(shaking head wildly) No, don't... think like that, make him angry, make him tell us... why, what we've done, why you... and them...(stammers) what about Sarah...

(forcefully pushing words out past a dry throat) Don't ask about Sarah! Please, Elias... stop before it's too late... think of your wife, what she said about you... we can get out of here if you... calm down... stay alive...

(raising the sound level slightly) Oh, calm down? You two are having the time of your lives. The anticipation should've set you off by now... it shouldn't be hard, then... But, oh no, the thought of your poor little lives getting extinguished in just 5 minutes... (chuckles softly, almost musically) 5 minutes and your precious little world crumbles into nothing. You see the problem is not Kaelie getting information, though I do appreciate her desperation; my real goal's just to show her the world as I like to see it: raw fear in an environment built from fear, and the two of you are my playthings now. Elias keeps trying too hard; a few desperate cries from those two might be good for another little while...

(eyes fixed on the Butcher, pleading voice rising) What do you want?! Why are you doing this?! (pauses, eyes scanning the room, desperate for a distraction or a chance) The picture... my wife... I have to tell her... if I don't... if I don't...

(voice shaking) Elias, don't... please... you can't save the... the pictures... or Sarah... nothing can save them from him... your wife... your children... (gulps) I'm not going to lose another person I've met in this... this hell, tell him whatever... (stares up at The Butcher, pleading eyes fixed on the red digital clock) Just don't make it worse, please...
